**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-04-23T21:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-cod-liver-oil-sources.10635/post-123229>

I have been buying the Green Pastures BLUE ICE Royal Butter Oil/Fermented Cod Liver Oil Blend product for fat soluble vitamins, and supplementing that with the Life Extension vitamin K product. Does anyone have a cheaper source for the fermented cod liver oil? With shipping, a single small container of the Green Pastures product is $60. That's a $720 yearly habit just for fat soluble vitamins. There really should be a cheaper way to get this.

### sooperb — 2014-04-30T10:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-cod-liver-oil-sources.10635/post-124011>

Mercola might do something.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-30T11:03:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-cod-liver-oil-sources.10635/post-124013>

AFAIK, Green Pastures is the only company in the world making a fermented CLO.

Is it any cheaper when not mixed with the butter oil? I can't recall whether it is or not over here. Because, of course, you can always include good quality butter in your diet even though it's not quite so rich a source of AA.

Is your reckoning based on a teaspoon a day or what? Since cost depends on how much you take, you could, even if you want to use it, use rather less but also supplement with D3 capsules.

### bionaut — 2014-04-30T11:14:59+0300

Does anyone have a cheaper source for the fermented cod liver oil? With shipping, a single small container of the Green Pastures product is $60. That's a $720 yearly habit just for fat soluble vitamins. There really should be a cheaper way to get this.

I couldn't get get cheaper than that for fclo. They have a sale the last week of the year if you want to stock up. I did use Max DHA from Jarrow and got decent results. Now I just eat canned sardines for lunch 4 times a week. They are 2.50 a can, wild caught with bone in. You are getting iodine, dha, EPA, proline and all of the cofactors in an electron dense package.

# Thread: I need help!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cmegan — 2014-04-24T01:03:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123243>

Hi!

Here is my question in a nutshell...how do other members who cannot tolerate bone broths (secondary to histamine and glutamate issues) get their collagen? I remember that Seahorse and I think BJK77 have these issues. Do you focus more on eating cuts of meat that are higher in collagen like lamb/beef shoulders and shanks? Anything else? Pork skins?

I was diagnosed several weeks ago with frozen shoulder and am truly suffering with pain and horrible nightly insomnia. I am exhausted. Dr. Kruse and Josh posted that is was basically a vasculitis issue and RO water and bone broth is the way to go. I was started to have bone broth three times per day (either lamb or fish heads/frame) and boom, 4 days into it I developed large welts all over my face, palpitations, etc etc etc. Now at least I understand why I never did well on GAPS or with winter slow cooked stews, sauerkraut, etc. I am seeing my GP next week and will have all new bloods done including MTHFR, but for now I need to understand how to get some collagen in my system! Or any other ideas about talking this painful syndrome! Thanks! Chrissy

### cmegan — 2014-04-24T01:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123244>

An interesting experience today related to this, I went to see the acupuncturist today for my shoulder...I've been seeing him in chinatown for about 25 years, he doesn't speak english and has never spoken to me. I just point to the part of my body that is ailing me and he assesses my tongue and pulse and gives me needles...he always helps me. When I went today and showed him my lack of ROM in my shoulder and suggested that I had pain, he called in one his assistants to interpret for him (which he's never done before!) She turned to me and said "he wants you to eat many many chicken wings"!

### cantweight — 2014-04-24T01:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123245>

That is so wild...I guess you need to eat connective tissue to heal connective tissue. Too bad you cant do the broth, a nice big bowl of pho with tendon could be so good for you!!

### SeaHorse — 2014-04-24T02:59:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123256>

I feel your pain cmegan…. I have to really limit the broths, but what I do is get pork hocks and cook them in water with bay leaf, onion and a clove, salt and pepper and then I eat them that same day, lots of cartilage and fat in the hocks…no reheating or long 5 hours cooking time this will cut down on the histamine and glutamate. I seem to be able to do this pretty regularly, but I don't have it every day. Also your idea of shin beef is right on…very good and with beef I'm happy to eat it raw still can't do that with pork. Pork skins from wellness are good too, but the heating part worries me a bit. Lamb parts are great.

Basically I try to eat my collagen instead of drink it.. As for your shoulder, what about buckets of RO water, a small amount of protein and lots of cartilage/fat for a week or so? ….do you have a red light you can use on your shoulder…although with dehydration that might be too much input.

Have you ever used Histame?…it can help to a degree. Best wishes.

### cmegan — 2014-04-24T03:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123259>

thanks seahorse this is all good advice! I have been buying my lamb from a man in TX and he was explaining to me that the shoulder is as high in collagen as are the shanks. I bought a lamb shoulder chop in WF tonight (cheaper then most cuts) and grilled it and chewed a lot on the bone after eating the meat...I think your idea of lots of RO water, little protein, and more cartilage/fat is great. I'm not quite sure where seafood fits into this scenario right now as it is low in collagen and I have been craving lamb chops and poultry skin. I will look into pork skins from Wellness. It was so funny at my sister's house for Easter this weekend my nephew pulled out a bag of pork rinds (I haven't had them since my old Atkins days!). It was so much fun to eat something that reminded me of potato chips without the carbs! I haven't used histame, I will look into it! I like your advice of eating your collagen instead of drinking it! Thank you....

### Inger — 2014-04-24T08:36:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123282>

Raw grass fed heart is awesome I would say, and very easy to eat too  remember to chew less

> Beef heart contains all essential amino acids, zinc, selenium and phosphorus. **It has more than double the elastin and collagen than other cuts of meat** and a highly concentrated source of coenzyme Q10, also known as CoQ10.

[http://www.livestrong.com/article/336563-is-beef-heart-healthy/](http://www.livestrong.com/article/336563-is-beef-heart-healthy/)

You have read the the gut-collagen blog too...? and seen Jacks advice to swallow pieces of raw "connective tissue" stuff....

[http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-1-gutcollagen-link/#idc-cover](http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-1-gutcollagen-link/#idc-cover)

I think I have the same issue as you Megan... my shoulder(s) is still not 100%. I am not surprised because I have done stupid things lately - ha!
My cure will be lots of grounding and sunshine, dips in the river as always, lots of raw heart - not much chewed - and also swallowing connective tissue and hardly chewable stuff and fish head smoothies and raw seafood and working on my mind

I love your acupuncturist - lucky you!

### cmegan — 2014-04-24T14:26:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123301>

Inger, I am always so impressed with your ability to eat raw...I can only do cerviche now, but after looking at your post I think I will try some raw lamb heart. I think the fact that I don't have to "chew" it will make it so much easier!!! I am so happy that spring is here, I live 5 minutes from the ocean and go there everyday...last night I stood with my feet in the water, it was so cold but it felt so good! Thank you for your advice! I hope your shoulder gets better too!

### SeaHorse — 2014-04-24T14:35:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123303>

Lamb heart is the easiest to start with….Sometimes I chop up heart, pour a bit of evo on it, salt, chopped herbs like cilantro, parsley and dill and a squeeze of lemon. As with the mhs and raw fish….lemon and cilantro go a long way to "normalizing" the taste until you can appreciate it for what it is (which is never with MHS, but always now with raw heart and beef) Bon Voyage!

# Thread: FLowchart/Poster for Quantum Model
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flowchart-poster-for-quantum-model.10638/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-04-24T01:58:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flowchart-poster-for-quantum-model.10638/post-123246>

guys
ive been trying to organise a visual flowchart/diagram to easily tie all concepts from the blog into one visual diagram/flowchart for ease of understanding for newcomers, after reading the comments in the help thread.

does anyone want to help/throw ideas around in this thread for a format?

i am thinking the three legged stool of water light and magnetism in the center, with arrows and lines between them indicating how they tie together

we could perhaps then have various threads emerging outward as things get more detailed...

anyways any help would be welcomed, ive been over a few ideas in my head and want to start working on it to help the community and get newbs to have a decent understanding on firts sight, which will save Jack the frustration of harping over the same stuff again and again, as this tends to slow the momentum inherent in this type of energetic learning

### Albert83BCN — 2014-04-24T02:33:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flowchart-poster-for-quantum-model.10638/post-123253>

I posted a reply on the other thread with an idea of mine that maybe is a little bit complex. There are some other easy alternatives, like this one I tried several months ago for a project we should do on the university regarding mindmaps and stuff like that. It's called PearlTrees and it allows to build a conceptual diagram (not a flow diagram in this case), the cool things are that its collaborative, it means that some other people from the forum might join and help to make grow the diagram, and also it allows to pickup resources from the net, that means that if you put two nodes like leptin and IL6 tied together, both can be an URL to wikipedia or even this site, so you do not only have the relationships but also some links to digg deeper.

This is the url if you want to check it out.
[http://www.pearltrees.com/](http://www.pearltrees.com/)

Needless to say, there're tons of similars tools on the net, the difficult task is to choose the one that suits better your needs, that includes cost. For me, the problem with this one is that it's not easy to embed in my own site. That's one of the reasons that I'd opt to develop my custom solution, but I guess some other people could be just fine with the features of such a tool as is.

### nonchalant — 2014-04-24T02:48:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flowchart-poster-for-quantum-model.10638/post-123255>

I was kicking around a pyramid design, like that ridiculous govnt. food pyramid, but updated.
3 legged stool on the bottom (largest portion) with shellfish, seaweed, fish, etc above.

But, except for the 3 leg stool, there is no one-size-fits-all prescription.

### SeaHorse — 2014-04-24T03:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flowchart-poster-for-quantum-model.10638/post-123257>

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-24T06:13:23+0300

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-04-24T09:41:48+0300

# Thread: Backwards on carb-timing.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Infinusss — 2014-04-24T05:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123275>

Anyone seen this, yet?

Dave says that it's not a good idea to not eat starchy carbs at all. He says that it can mess up your body's mucus production in your stomach and eyes. He also says that you should eat your carbs at night, because if you eat your cabs in the AM, you will experience a roller coaster or blood sugar spikes throughout the day and have crashes. He also says that eating carbs at night can help some people sleep better, because you experience the increased blood sugar while you sleep which can help feed your brain and help with nighttime recovery.

Has anyone ever experienced any of what he's talking about? I haven't had a starchy carb in 6 weeks, and I HIIT really hard 3 times a week. I seem to have no problem without carbs, and I sleep just fine. I did the leptin reset, so that could have something to do with it. Maybe I'm getting better at fat burning thanks to our resident doc.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-24T06:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123276>

Bro science.........

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-24T10:29:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123288>

> [Infinusss said:](/goto/post?id=123275)
>
> Anyone seen this, yet?
>
>
> Dave says that it's not a good idea to not eat starchy carbs at all.

I find I can't listen to Dave any more. But I did hear him mention this "safe starch" ideology when he was talking to a ketogenic diet expert -- an academic and a man who works with special forces soldiers and all sorts of people. The expert pretty much said: I'm sorry to hear about your experience, Dave, maybe you didn't do it right. I don't think Dave heard.

It seems to me that Dave thinks that his experience bears out this notion that Paul Jaminet has come up with. It turns out he was ill after trying what he calls an "Eskimo Diet". Now he thinks he knows why. AFAICT, he was eating mostly lamb and one small portion of green vegetables a day. An Eskimo Diet? Not many sheep within the Arctic Circle.

You can't just take an American diet and drop the veg out and _call_ it an Eskimo diet. It's more complex than that.

### Shijin13 — 2014-04-24T18:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123322>

So one question - what about people who don't do well on a keto diet - Like Jeremy? b/c it degrades their redox potential?

Iv'e added more carbs back in and BAM my redox potential has increased rapidly. hair growth, nail growth, circadian timing has returned where I'm waking up earlier w/o an alarm clock again (when I was deep keto these things disappeared)

that said - I'm not eating 100 grams of carbs either. I've just upped my carbs. But I've also tried to go back to the LR BAB and when I do that I BONK big time. I'm doing better for breakfast having a combo of straight seafood, or a fat/carb combo. protein isn't really on my radar for most of my meals. Fat is still the primary driver and adding a small amt of carbs (beyond greens) at each meal has been awesome for me.

I felt best and had the most success when I was following the Post Leptin Rx, after doing the Leptin Reset for almost 2yrs. Going back to what was working last year... My N=1 is different. but that doesn't mean I'm not hanging out of ketosis either...

### Infinusss — 2014-04-24T18:56:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123330>

>
> that said - I'm not eating 100 grams of carbs either. I've just upped my carbs. But I've also tried to go back to the LR BAB and when I do that I BONK big time. I'm doing better for breakfast having a combo of straight seafood, or a fat/carb combo. protein isn't really on my radar for most of my meals. Fat is still the primary driver and adding a small amt of carbs (beyond greens) at each meal has been awesome for me.
>
> I felt best and had the most success when I was following the Post Leptin Rx, after doing the Leptin Reset for almost 2yrs. Going back to what was working last year... My N=1 is different. but that doesn't mean I'm not hanging out of ketosis either...

Do you count just starchy carbs or all carbs? For instance, an avocado has about 17g carbs, but it's not starchy. I haven't had a starchy carb in 6 weeks, but I do get carbs from green veggies, eggs and avocados. I switched away from the leptin reset after 6 weeks and I'm doing the post leptin Rx now, but I still haven't added in starchy carbs at all. Maybe I'll try a cup of white rice in the morning with my eggs and avocado and see what happens.

### NeilBB — 2014-04-24T18:59:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123331>

> [Infinusss said:](/goto/post?id=123330)
>
> For instance, an avocado has about 17g carbs, .

Mostly indigestible fiber. About 14 of 17 grams. From a digestible macronutrient standpoint, for what its worth, avocados are almost all fat. I eat them constantly.

### cantweight — 2014-04-24T19:24:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123334>

> [Infinusss said:](/goto/post?id=123330)
>
> Do you count just starchy carbs or all carbs? For instance, an avocado has about 17g carbs, but it's not starchy. I haven't had a starchy carb in 6 weeks, but I do get carbs from green veggies, eggs and avocados. I switched away from the leptin reset after 6 weeks and I'm doing the post leptin Rx now, but I still haven't added in starchy carbs at all. Maybe I'll try a cup of white rice in the morning with my eggs and avocado and see what happens.

I think where I screwed up was counting all carbs. I got into ketosis in a day or two going by urine strips. Kept my carbs in the 10-15 range and they consisted of greens, veggies and nuts. Found out from other people with big time keto weight loss success that they were only counting starches...sweet potato, coconut flour stuff like that. I hit mega mega walls with my low carb. Had problems with dry eyes like Dave Asprey mentioned, ammonia urine...my inflammation rose and my weight did too.

Finally loosening the reigns and seem to be responding quite well. White rice I would only do if it was cooked in bone broth and cooled before eating....and I'd save it for the evening meal. And only on occasion....

### Shijin13 — 2014-04-24T19:35:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123336>

I switched away from the leptin reset after 6 weeks and I'm doing the post leptin Rx now, but I still haven't added in starchy carbs at all. Maybe I'll try a cup of white rice in the morning with my eggs and avocado and see what happens.

I look at all carbs. I may have a 1/2 sweet potato, or winter squash once a week during fall /winter (though I was craving them like mad). I didn't do well dropping everything. and greens - as much as I was eating them - weren't sustaining me, even when in ketosis. Since adding berries and a little honey into my diet daily things have bounced back quickly. I did try sushi one day last week at dinner - and the next day I was go-go-go. crazy thing - even add those items I haven't bounced out of ketosis.

N=1 staying seasonal but making sure I get a bit more carbs than before.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-24T19:58:26+0300

Up to you, but Jack says:

> I do not eat any rice because its miRNA directly affects our DNA expression.

[http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/)

Not trying to persuade you to do anything you don't want, but as you're here …

For myself I guess if I felt like a handful of tortilla chips or an oatcake or whatever I'd go ahead. What passes me by is the notion that I should _need_ starch. There seems to be nothing to back that up. I think I'd prefer something like potatoes fried in goose fat to white rice anyway.

### Infinusss — 2014-04-24T23:35:55+0300

Tortilla chips? I've seen Jack, on more than one occasion, say that corn is a no-no. He also said to stay away from regular potatoes, as well. Man, that kills my carb options, and now, no white rice? Yikes.

### Infinusss — 2014-04-25T00:12:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123362>

> [Gagnrad said:](/goto/post?id=123288)

The expert pretty much said: I'm sorry to hear about your experience, Dave, maybe you didn't do it right. I don't think Dave heard.
>
> It seems to me that Dave thinks that his experience bears out this notion that Paul Jaminet has come up with. It turns out he was ill after trying what he calls an "Eskimo Diet". Now he thinks he knows why. AFAICT, he was eating mostly lamb and one small portion of green vegetables a day. An Eskimo Diet? Not many sheep within the Arctic Circle.
>
> You can't just take an American diet and drop the veg out and _call_ it an Eskimo diet. It's more complex than that.

Yeah, I appreciate some of the work the Asprey has done, and some of his ideas, but he seems to go overboard with some of his views. He seems to have good intentions, but he really is almost a hypochondriac. I've never heard of someone being so crippled by minute amounts of mycotoxins and other "contaminants." I'm not saying that those things don't affect him, but I just don't believe that that is the case for most people. He has so many people over at his site freaking out about the smallest, teeny tiny details on food. I tried living that way for a while, but everyone in my family wanted to murder me after a few weeks. No garlic, no onions, no black pepper, most spices and herbs are out, no nightshades, don't cook with butter or EVOO, don't heat eggs past rare (no scrambled eggs or omelets). I just couldn't do it anymore.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-25T08:50:36+0300

Yup, he's against all grains. He's OK with sweet potatoes. He had the book _Sweet Potato Power_ linked at one time. They turn up in the cookbook in a few places.

And, of course, he eats fruit in season. I think he said his own carbohydrate consumption went up to 250g in high summer.

### ssj3 — 2014-04-25T11:38:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/backwards-on-carb-timing.10639/post-123418>

> Bro science.........

Having to eat carbs or meal timing?

From n=1 perspective, if I am going through a period where I am consuming carbs, timing them based on when I workout has a noticeable beneficial affects on body fat levels, energy and recovery compared to just consuming at all/any points of the day.

# Thread: Thoughts that made me.........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-24T06:30:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-123279>

Become a skeptic; blind belief in any authority is the greatest enemy of truth. The independent mind is built not on what it believes, but in how it thinks. Night.........

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-24T13:15:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-123298>

No commitment, no results. Commitment is an all or none phenomena. Commitment is disciplined focused; without focus, you only have dreams and hopes, but no plan for success.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-25T11:14:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-123415>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=123347)
>
> I see Einstein agrees with you

I'm not sure how far I do. You can only push it so far. We make use of so much information every day that each person simply _couldn't_ check it all for himself: he'd never get anything done.

If an electrician said to you, "Don't touch that wire!" it would be irrational not to give some weight to the authority with which he was speaking.

A smell of burning fills the air
Incautious Sue's longer there ...

These kind of issues have been debated back and forth for a very long time, and the debates get so technical that people are still arguing about what William James said WRT to W. K. Clifford in the 1870s. "Depends what's meant by 'belief'" and so on:

[http://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/belief/2010/nov/19/william-james-evidence-daleks-faith](http://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/belief/2010/nov/19/william-james-evidence-daleks-faith)

### Inger — 2014-04-25T11:24:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-123416>

> No commitment, no results. Commitment is an all or none phenomena. Commitment is disciplined focused; without focus, you only have dreams and hopes, but no plan for success.

love it!

### CK in LA — 2014-07-20T21:34:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-134998>

I find that the development of our inner authority requires painstaking attention on the conditioning that obscures our capacity to be in the unknown or cutting edge of learning what we need to know; holding in abeyance the known for new potentials. Courage can mean so many things depending on what we need to sacrifice. For me at this time, courage would mean enduring or holding the tension between the constructive aims of the psyche and the destructive forces that enforce the status quo.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-21T00:08:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-135008>

I like that perspective.........

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-21T00:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-135017>

Here is our irony: Man is the only creature intelligent enough to think themself to death................

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-21T00:55:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-135018>

And man is the only creature that can convince themselves that "all progress" has to be "all good" in "all circumstances".

Bad thinking is human mind candy.

### sjoshua — 2014-07-21T20:23:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-135101>

A lesson that I learned from my 3rd grade teacher, and has stuck with me my whole life: Can't means won't.

### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-02-17T17:27:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-155905>

My company commander in Navy boot camp loved to do, and make his recruits do push ups. He made us say "THANK YOU FOR BUILDING MY MIND AND BODY SIR" prior to us getting off the deck. I still use that today as one of my main influences when coaching young people!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-20T04:25:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-156106>

"Without focus, you only have dreams and hopes"
The reverse is also true - without dreams you don't have focus.

I believe brain chemistry can have a profound effect here -
Without a healthy brain chemistry, you lose the ability to dream.

The feeling you get from that dream is what gives determination, focus
and results.
Healing your brain chemistry gives you your dreams back.
I think a lot of people settle for less because they have lost this ability to dream, which fuels the drive.
I say this because I am one of them.
And stimulant medication is NOT the answer.

That's what my doctor prescribed me, kept escalating the dose, and now I'm addicted to the @#$% shit, even though it barely does a thing anymore.

That's my dream - to find that "feeling" again WITHOUT medication.
That rush that pushes you towards that goal like a locomotive.
I think I just need someone to tell me that its possible.

### caroline — 2015-02-20T09:53:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-that-made-me.10640/post-156126>

Dream big ....or stay home Mike! you got nothing to lose - but everything to gain.

we are your support team, your cheer leaders, a shoulder to lean on ...and you are ours.

We are all in this together!

**Posts:** 1

### lioness7 — 2014-04-24T07:28:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-than-a-minute-to-strangle-a-child.10641/post-123281>

[http://parentsforwindowblindsafety.org/](http://parentsforwindowblindsafety.org/)

I am posting this public service announcement for anyone on the forum with small children as well as to share with others they know. Though I was aware of the danger when my kids were younger, I never heard the stories until recently. Two children a week die or become severely incapacitated from something so simply prevented through awareness.I hope that you all share this.

# Thread: Another quantum poem:  OCEANS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-quantum-poem-oceans.10642/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SeaHorse — 2014-04-24T16:02:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-quantum-poem-oceans.10642/post-123308>

I have a feeling that my boat
has struck,
down there in the depths,
against a great thing.

And nothing happens!
Nothing…Silence…Waves…

\--Nothing happen? Or has
everything happened, and are
we standing now, quietly,
in the new life?

Juan Ramon Jimenez

translated by Robert Bly

### sooperb — 2014-04-25T11:00:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-quantum-poem-oceans.10642/post-123408>

Isn't that a lovely challenging concept? I like it

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-converstation.10643/post-123323>

Jack mentioned on the recent Ameer Rosic podcast that he'd posted thermographic pictures of a teenager before and after a 15 min conversation on an iPhone 5S that showed a huge change in blood flow to the affected side of the head.

My job compels me to spend a lot of time on the phone (working on changing that) frequent 1-1.5 hour conference calls etc.

I never hold the phone up to my head for those, but nearly always (until recently) have used wired headset, most recently with those little ferrite beads on the cables, now trying to get away from that and working on alternatives. Mostly use speakerphone now when possible. Looking for new job...

Usually after a day spent at computer / on phone I feel pretty beat up and drained. Never bad headaches but just edgy and unhappy.

I don't think you are imagining it at all. Mobile phones are very dangerous tools.

RobH

... although there's a similar pair of images that show someone's brain after sitting still and after taking a walk. _There_ I think the red is supposed to show that there's more brain activity so you should get moving. Debatable, I think: there's nothing _wrong_ with sitting still, although sure you shouldn't do it all the time and most of us probably could do with more walking. The extent to which you can prove something with an image .... but they do have this impact factor.

You really need more context to go with the image. Just how hot did the red areas get? Did the heating cause any damage? and so on.

No, I haven't heard of BIO DOT.

The video someone posted here of an woman in U.S. who had a tumour in her breast the same size and shape as her phone -- she used to carry it there -- was pretty spooky.

On computers -- is yours grounded? Do you ground yourself when you use it?

On sleep -- I take it you're sleeping in a darkened room and not using light with the blue part of the spectrum in after dark, taking magnesium, etc. Again, how about grounding? Have you tried a grounding bedsheet (not cheap I know)?

### lubylouUK — 2014-04-26T16:30:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-converstation.10643/post-123511>

Hi is that link to the grounding sheet just for example or is that a good one to get?

I'm just in the midst of planning on building a hut at the end of my garden to sleep in. ...

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-27T13:30:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-converstation.10643/post-123563>

> [lubylouUK said:](/goto/post?id=123511)
>
> Hi is that link to the grounding sheet just for example or is that a good one to get?
>
> I'm just in the midst of planning on building a hut at the end of my garden to sleep in. ...

AFAIK, all the sheets are made for the company Clint Ober himself owns and are only ones currently on the market. That particular one is the half-sheet. I guess the "recovery bags" would be a nice option. There's also a full sheet. But both those are more expensive still. The half-sheet seems to be all that's necessary.

There's also a little mouse-pad made from rubber with carbon in. It's intended for use under a mouse or under your bare feet while you sit. That's a fair bit cheaper:

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Original-Barefoot-Earthing®-Grounding-textured/dp/B00F8I62T2/](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Original-Barefoot-Earthing%C2%AE-Grounding-textured/dp/B00F8I62T2/)

I have slept with one of those under my thigh. It sort of "stuck" to it a little -- like a rubber-scution cup! -- but that kind of worked.

Some people might want to experiment with what 20 to 40 minutes of grounding will do for them before splashing out. You can do that with nothing just barefoot on the grass -- perhaps water it well first. Or wade in the sea, if you can, which is even better.

Ober seems to start everyone who approaches him with the sheet, though. It may be that it is more helpful when you are asleep, or that it helps people who can't sleep well to sleep. Partly, though, I think, it's just that it's a good way to get in plenty of time. The effect seems to be cumulative: the more time earthed, the better.

I did buy the sheet (well, half-sheet), because although it was costly and I have no particular problem the case made in the book for earthing seems to me to be overwhelming.

### lubylouUK — 2014-04-27T14:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-converstation.10643/post-123578>

Great thank you! I need all the healing time I can get. I'm really struggling with adrenal problems. after 2 days of living a fairly normal life I have to pay the price with 4 days resting!

I did put my feet in the sea yesterday but I crashed badly last night. It was getting to the beach that was too much. Just sat in the car. Nothing special. Back to square one! Agh.....

I just really hope this is going to help my adrenal problems. I'm running out of ideas!

PS do you have to get a wire running from the sheet out of the window and into the ground?

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-27T16:41:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-converstation.10643/post-123582>

> [lubylouUK said:](/goto/post?id=123578)
>
> PS do you have to get a wire running from the sheet out of the window and into the ground?

No, but it's probably best.

The earth socket on a wall plug-socket should go to earth anyway. (The socket doesn't have to be switched on: it's just a roundabout way of linking to the ground.) However, there can be a problem with "dirty electricity" leaking back. And occasionally the earth hasn't been connected. This, I guess, is mostly a function of how old the wiring in a house is and how competent its installer. It's said that "dirty electricity" can be a problem for very electro-sensitive people.

In short, if it's physically possible to connect the sheet individually and directly straight into the ground, it's the better option, but if not there's the mains. Evidently, that worked very well for the _Tour de France_ cyclists in hotel rooms who couldn't install permanent connections.

Here's the rod and cable -- two options, which look very similar, both coming from BEP:

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Barefoot-ERC550-Earthing-and-Grounding/dp/B007819M1C/](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Barefoot-ERC550-Earthing-and-Grounding/dp/B007819M1C/)

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Earthing®-Connectors-adapters-connection-Earthing/dp/B00GKNIVIO/](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Earthing®-Connectors-adapters-connection-Earthing/dp/B00GKNIVIO/)

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-28T15:13:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-converstation.10643/post-123660>

> [lubylouUK said:](/goto/post?id=123650)
>
> Great. Thanks. I have been siting bare foot outside on the wet ground for last 2 days and I must say I do feel a lot better! Less stressed! Calm and steady.

That's great!

> The earthing sheet isn't magnetic is it? Would a magnetic one be better? Is that what Jack is getting at? I've seen the word magnetico used a few times. That's something else isn't it?

Yes, it's something else. It's a mattress. Those really are expensive, and have to be imported from the U.S., so I haven't looked into it. If I understood right, the mattress replicates the Earth's magnetic field, which you're remote from when not sleeping on, or very near, the ground. I guess that can be a help for some people, as most people sleep on the second floor (or higher) nowadays. Maybe it wouldn't be necessary for you, if you're moving to a sleeping-hut in the garden, as you said. I don't know for sure.

There's information on the site on them:

[http://jackkruse.com/magnetico/](http://jackkruse.com/magnetico/)

> THank you so much for your input. It's really helping!

I'm glad.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-28T21:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-converstation.10643/post-123745>

> [lubylouUK said:](/goto/post?id=123736)
>
> I've had a quick read. I think it's saying the magnetico is far superior to the earths own energy field!

I see what you mean.

I listened to the video first. The explanation comes at about 2:28. There Jack says:

> The pad actually limits Brownian motion so that the native electromagnetic force that's found on the Earth can correctly read my RNA and DNA, so that they can be expressed appropriately to keep me well.

That sounds rather different. There the pad seems to be allowing the Earth to work as it should.

However, the text on that same page has:

> The problem is the Earth’s field is severely depleted – it has been reduced to one half of a gauss.

I went to the Magnetico site and found this:

> Since 1829, scientists have been able to accurately measure the Earth’s magnetic field and have recorded a decline of its strength. Today the magnetic field of the Earth is measured between 0.3 and 0.6 gauss (a unit of measurement of magnetic fields). It is estimated that the Earth’s magnetic field 4,000 years ago was anywhere between 2.5 and 5.0 gauss. That is a decrease of 80% or more.

I don't know whether that's true. There was a recent discussion on these very boards on how far one could rely on "authority". I should think it is likely true. I suppose one could research this and try to verify that for oneself rather than taking it on trust.

It's interesting.

If the pad merely lets the Earth "work as it should", as I put it, then it's putting things back to how they were, restoring the _status quo ante_, that was there before non-native EMF, sleeping high up or whatever else was going on -- making things how they were throughout our evolutionary history.

If the Earth's magnetic field has declined, then, again, the Magnetico is putting things back to how they were in the past. But the whole thing becomes more dramatic -- and possibly more important.

I don't know. The smallest, cheapest Magnetico pad is $510. That's around £300. You'd have to add shipping and for people in the EU there are pretty hefty EU import charges, too. I'd have to hope these weren't a necessity.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-28T22:53:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-converstation.10643/post-123759>

It is true but not because of what he has said.........When the ionosphere has 2 billion times the energy in it.......it draws electrons to it and away from you.........so in effect we now live on a planet that has no magnetic field. IT chronically pulls it out every where. The key metric is to find a pocket where the electron loss is not as bad and has all the other things to deliver electrons to the mitochondria well.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-29T11:29:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-converstation.10643/post-123840>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=123759)
>
> It is true but not because of what he has said.........When the ionosphere has 2 billion times the energy in it.......it draws electrons to it and away from you.........so in effect we now live on a planet that has no magnetic field. IT chronically pulls it out every where. The key metric is to find a pocket where the electron loss is not as bad and has all the other things to deliver electrons to the mitochondria well.

IT? Information Technology?

I hadn't seen what he (the person behind the Magnetico site, I take it) said. Did he say that it could be a sign that the earth's polarity is about to flip?

Of course, as he lays it out on the page I saw the decline depends on the accuracy of the equipment used and its operators' competence now, and in the past back to 1826. None of us is in a position to check that. And that's a very small slice of time. But it can be combined with other observations and sources of evidence, of course.

Hello I had an hour long converstation on my mobile the other day and I have been feeling really ill since. I have adrenal fatigue. I have been having headaches and feeling pretty bad since. Does anyone else experience this? Am I imagining it??

### Amanda J — 2014-05-05T13:10:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-conversation.10644/post-125257>

Depends on various factors, which company's mobile do you use, is it summer time, had the mobile phone's speaker been heating up while you were on call, did you stretch your self at night and were sleep deprived if so some rest and medication can help. Did you check your body temperature.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-05T14:43:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-conversation.10644/post-125269>

^^^^Not It does not depend upon those factors. It is 100% tied to signal and how close it is to your head. The cell phone always heats up as a bidirectional microwave walkie talkie

### Amanda J — 2014-05-05T15:56:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-conversation.10644/post-125278>

yes those bad microwaves can harm your brain. But its the heat which hurts more.

### bionaut — 2014-05-05T20:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effects-of-long-mobile-conversation.10644/post-125323>

Amanda

> [Amanda J said:](/goto/post?id=125278)
>
> yes those bad microwaves can harm your brain. But its the heat which hurts more.

there is a blog called the emf series

[http://jackkruse.com/emf-1-does-your-rolex-work/](http://jackkruse.com/emf-1-does-your-rolex-work/)

It is a series that covers emf and how it is a barrier to optimal. These blogs can be difficult to digest but I promise you that by the end you will be rewarded with a new understanding of biology as it relates to physics. A lot of us on this site have made this journey. Once you get your feet wet and start asking better questions in lieu of giving answers a lot becomes clearer. Give the blogs a go before you give too much advice here, many of the thoughts of the forum members are formed using a physics context. Please also check out the comments on the blogs as they answer many questions that we've all had.

# Thread: Turning off the juice....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-off-the-juice.10645/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RobH — 2014-04-24T18:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-off-the-juice.10645/post-123327>

For months I've been occasionally and intermittently (not often enough) turning off breakers to our bedroom at night. This has been a huge pain (thus the occasionally and intermittently) because of the location of our breaker box -- outside, around the house, through bushes, etc.

Had an electrician come out yesterday and install a 4-pole "contactor" that allows me to cut power to 3 circuits (the one feeding master bedroom / bath) area by simply flipping a cute little red switch. The switch is in master bath about 15 feet from bed (hope that's not too close...), and has MC (metal clad) shielded cable running to it. Now we can cut 3 circuits by just flipping a single switch in a convenient place. The hardware supposedly was less than $100 and it was about 2 hours of electrician time to install (waiting for bill). Wish I would have done it months ago.

My wife reports great sleep in a last night.  I slept pretty well after getting to bed at 2:30 after a shift.

The confounding factor is that the other thing the electricians were hooking up was the infrared sauna.... She got in that before bed and said it was wonderful.

I've messed with 2 variables at once so not such a good experimentalist I guess.

If you haven't looked into having something like this installed -- you should. It is very convenient and thus enhances compliance....

Highly recommended.

RobH

### SeaHorse — 2014-04-24T20:39:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-off-the-juice.10645/post-123340>

We did the same thing and got a kill switch for our bedroom/bath/hallway around Christmas time and along with the magnetico our sleep has been super great since….

# Thread: Fluid and Electrolytes on Low Carb Diets
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 61
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-04-25T03:41:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-123370>

After reading appropriate sections of the book "The Art and Science of Low Carbohydrate Living" I am now aware of the fact that low carb diets tend to be natriuretic: the kidneys dump sodium in urine, and the body contracts circulating volume. It is the contracted volume that causes some people to end up with extreme fatigue. As this gets worse, the body starts to sacrifice some of its intracellular potassium, leading to cardiac dysrhthmias and muscle cramps. You start losing muscle too.

Now if you are overweight, hypertensive, and holding lots of water weight, then these effects might actually be a virtue. But if you are like me - very lean and totally normal water retention and blood pressure - you get to see the ugly sides of this problem. I have all of the symptoms described above. And the real shocker was that my problems controlling blood glucose after eating carbs may in part reflect a sodium deficiency. I can totally correct my glucose response to carbs for a few hours after ingesting enough sodium. Unfortunately, after a few hours I disregulate glucose again.

The recommendation made in "The Art and Science of Carbohydrate Living" is for people on a low carb diets to take five grams of sodium a day. That's about two and one half teaspoons of sea salt, and that is a lot of salt. What the book does not make clear is what is the amount of \*fluid\* you should be taking together with that salt? Has anyone seen guidelines for this?

Since part of the problem here is the body is contracting fluid volume in order to hang onto sodium, it seems that the correction to this problem would require you to correctly balance both your sodium intake as well as the amount of fluid you are taking.

I have tried the five grams of sodium for the last few days, and my most severe symptoms resolve. But my _extreme_ fatigue remains. I am now convinced I have a severe electrolyte management problem. What I really need is to identify a doctor who specializes in low carb and who is very good at managing these electrolyte issues. If anyone has a referral I would love to have that. I have exhausted my local resources, and every doctor I have including my endocrinologist wants to solve this problem by having me take more carbs.

### JMO — 2014-04-25T03:52:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-123371>

In the book, I think they recommend having bone broths daily to overcome this problem. I've had this problem too. Coconut water seems to help for me. I prob wasn't eating seafood enough in the beginning. Also, I now take trace minerals.

### persistence — 2014-04-25T03:58:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-123373>

> [JMO said:](/goto/post?id=123371)
>
> In the book, I think they recommend having bone broths daily to overcome this problem. I've had this problem too. Coconut water seems to help for me. I prob wasn't eating seafood enough in the beginning. Also, I now take trace minerals.

I'm taking 16 oz of water with 1/2 teaspoon of salt, five times a day. But I am wondering if that is the right fluid balance. I want to get the water and the sodium together, to balance those two critical elements in the right proportions.

Coconut water has very little sodium but massive amounts of potassium. This is actually one of the questions I have. Should I be taking extra potassium together with the sodium? My blood tests typically show potassium at 4, but this is the free potassium in the blood, and what we should care about is potassium inside the cell. I really don't understand the issues here well enough to feel comfortable supplementing potassium yet. I think I need some professional help figuring this all out.

How are you getting the trace minerals? What is the product name, and if you have some rough recollection of quantities of the key electrolytes in that it would be good to see.

Do you feel all of your symptoms have been corrected after doing this?

This diet has just about killed me. Good thing I am persistent or I think I would have stuck head in oven a long time ago.

### JMO — 2014-04-25T04:23:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-123375>

My DO gave me NutraMedix Trace Minerals and QMP (Quinton Marine Plasma) Isotonic. I also sometimes mix Humic Minerals (vital Earth) in my water. The coconut water just makes me feel better. I feel sooo much better now. I was prob really dehydrated. Bone broths are great too!

### JMO — 2014-04-25T04:32:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-123376>

Also, Dr. Tim recomended to me Synerplex Electrolyte Concentrate during a consult.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-26T11:59:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-123492>

There's a much-linked post of Dr. Eades's on this, persistence.

I don't know that this is an effect of low-carb diets, so much as an effect that happens when you _start_ them. As Mike Eades puts it:

> The good news is that it’s great to get rid of the excess fluid but it comes at a cost, which is the bad news. As the excess fluid goes, it takes with it sodium an extremely important electrolyte.

I think people should adjust after awhile. It's certainly a fact that hunting peoples seem to have consumed relatively little salt. Stefansson, while staying with Eskimos, once put salt in a stew to make sure no-one else would take any: he knew they couldn't stand the taste. Salt becomes vital when people switch to a farming lifestyle: agricultural settlements seem to be, as it were, predicated on deposits of salt; it seems you need it for making HCL for digesting the excess carbohydrate.

But, sure, not enough salt can be a problem on transitioning.

The Eades prescribe potassium to all the patients they put on a low-carb diet:

[http://www.proteinpower.com/drmike/...ks-for-starting-or-restarting-low-carb-pt-ii/](http://www.proteinpower.com/drmike/saturated-fat/tips-tricks-for-starting-or-restarting-low-carb-pt-ii/)

Jack says not to add salt to water -- to salt your food instead. I'm not entirely sure why, but it seems he says that pure water hydrates better. I think this comes in a blog he did on water and hydration. I'd have to read it again, and closely, but as I recall he said that. It'll be in one of the blogs posted over the last three or four months.

### persistence — 2014-04-26T21:54:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-123540>

> [Gagnrad said:](/goto/post?id=123492)
>
> There's a much-linked post of Dr. Eades's on this, persistence.
>
> I don't know that this is an effect of low-carb diets, so much as an effect that happens when you _start_ them. As Mike Eades puts it:

The research literature I have seen so far suggests that this is a persistent diuretic effect of low carb diets. It is seen most clearly when you start the diet because many overweight people retain a large amount of fluid, and the dramatic lowering of the fluid levels results in large weight loss early in the diet. Eventually you get to some steady state level, but the diet itself is an ongoing diuretic.

> I think people should adjust after awhile. It's certainly a fact that hunting peoples seem to have consumed relatively little salt. Stefansson, while staying with Eskimos, once put salt in a stew to make sure no-one else would take any: he knew they couldn't stand the taste. Salt becomes vital when people switch to a farming lifestyle: agricultural settlements seem to be, as it were, predicated on deposits of salt; it seems you need it for making HCL for digesting the excess carbohydrate.
>
> But, sure, not enough salt can be a problem on transitioning.
>
> The Eades prescribe potassium to all the patients they put on a low-carb diet:
>
> [http://www.proteinpower.com/drmike/...ks-for-starting-or-restarting-low-carb-pt-ii/](http://www.proteinpower.com/drmike/saturated-fat/tips-tricks-for-starting-or-restarting-low-carb-pt-ii/)

That links recommends taking extra sodium as well. If you are shedding sodium and only supplement potassium you are going to screw up your electrolytes even more. The research I read supplemented 5 grams of sodium and 1 gram of potassium per day, and I do not understand why that ratio.

In my case, I think I need to be under supervision of a real doctor who understands these issues. It's getting clearer to me now that most of my problems relate to electrolyte imbalances. I tried to take the five grams of sodium (via celtic sea salt) / one gram of potassium (via potassium chloride) per day, and now that pushes my sodium too high. I am simply not understanding how to get back into balance, and maybe there is some other issue contributing to the problem. I think these problems are bigger than my ability to solve them.

I found a local nephrologist (a specialization that should be pretty expert in electrolyte issues...) who runs a metabolic clinic. So maybe the combination of electrolyte and diet expertise will help me figure this out. If this guy starts pushing high carbs, then what to do...? I think my endocrinologist is starting to think about electrolytes now as well and is starting to run her own tests.

> Jack says not to add salt to water -- to salt your food instead. I'm not entirely sure why, but it seems he says that pure water hydrates better. I think this comes in a blog he did on water and hydration. I'd have to read it again, and closely, but as I recall he said that. It'll be in one of the blogs posted over the last three or four months.

In [Redox RX](http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/) at point 7 there is the passage:

"Avoid any exogenous salts in your drinking water if you can. This is not an axiomatic rule. Why? Salts. In addition to metals, salts can also conduct electricity. In cells we do not want a salt disturbing semiconducting flow of electrons. This creates loss of efficiency for energy transfers because it creates a smaller exclusion zone in water in a cell. In salt electric conduction, there are no free electrons moving, so the conductivity depends on the specific ions used in the salt. When a salt is in water, it is either melting or dissolving, so that the ions of the salt are free to move to conduct the current. Their atomic sizes however limit coherent conduction with 100% efficiency that is seen in a semiconductor. K+ is the key element in life’s equation with water to create a semiconductor that has the same properties we see in a topologic insulators."

I don't understand the point here regarding mixing with water. Why would it be okay to take the salt with food but not okay to take it with water? Either way the salt will end up in blood serum in the same form?

### yewwei.tan — 2014-04-29T06:23:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-123823>

> [persistence said:](/goto/post?id=123540)
>
> In [Redox RX](http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/) at point 7 there is the passage:
>
> "Avoid any exogenous salts in your drinking water if you can. This is not an axiomatic rule. Why? Salts. In addition to metals, salts can also conduct electricity. In cells we do not want a salt disturbing semiconducting flow of electrons. This creates loss of efficiency for energy transfers because it creates a smaller exclusion zone in water in a cell. In salt electric conduction, there are no free electrons moving, so the conductivity depends on the specific ions used in the salt. When a salt is in water, it is either melting or dissolving, so that the ions of the salt are free to move to conduct the current. Their atomic sizes however limit coherent conduction with 100% efficiency that is seen in a semiconductor. K+ is the key element in life’s equation with water to create a semiconductor that has the same properties we see in a topologic insulators."
>
> I don't understand the point here regarding mixing with water. Why would it be okay to take the salt with food but not okay to take it with water? Either way the salt will end up in blood serum in the same form?

EZ water tends to stay in its charge separated state until something comes along and disrupts that state. It's possible for your body to uptake the EZ water that you drink into the cell cytosol before it loses all of its EZ structure. See Pollack's 'Fourth Phase of Water' book for more info.

It's not an axiomatic rule because your body has its own means of structuring water to create EZ within a cell. The May 2014 webinar showed us exactly how that happens within neurons.

### persistence — 2014-04-29T23:11:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-123941>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=123935)
>
> "The research literature I have seen so far suggests that this is a persistent diuretic effect of low carb diets." Really? So do high carb diets.......this is why diabetics have polyuria as a major symptom. The effect is tied to a LOSS OF CHARGE EVERYWHERE IN THE CELL!!!

I guess there are multiple ways to end up with polyuria (excess urination). For example: diabetes mellitus, adrenal tumor, diabetes insipidus, etc.

However, outside of those disease conditions, more carbs generally means more water weight, not less.

Gary Taubes shows the science in "Good Calories, Bad Calories" that high carbohydrate diets cause kidneys to retain sodium. Higher sodium means your body retains more water in order to balance out the sodium concentration.

As carbs are reduced in the diet, your kidneys release excess sodium and water, and that is why low carb diets produce the initial large weight loss: the body uses up glycogen (tightly bound with water) and the body downregulates sodium and fluid volumes.

You are right that specific diseases change those results, but I'm not clear on why that helps to understand the basic diuretic effects of high versus low carb in otherwise healthy people.

> When a cell membrane loses charge it can not do what it should anywhere. This is why being too low or two high is bad........same key opens both doors.

This part is just outside my pay grade. I just don't have the foundation to understand the claim or comment.

### Bill1 — 2015-07-04T05:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-170642>

The RDA (_recommended disease allowance_) for K+ is 3 grams. Just 20 mins of a brisk walk uses 3 grams of K+. So you'll need 6 grams to be even. That would mean you'll need to eat about 20 pounds of shrimp in one meal.

It's fairly difficult to the optimal amounts of electrolytes - (_let alone osmolytes_) from food.

,

Well I'm not sure how much of this will apply to you but my problems started 6yrs ago, I got a bad virus and ever since problems like gastroparesis CFS POTS dry mouth etc have been getting worse. I have only recently connected this to the virus as I before thought it was from gastrointestinal issues. So the theory coming out from recent studies is that the virus causes cfs etc by fucking up your immune system so that antibodies are produced. I speculated that my problems where at adrenergic and muscarinic receptors due to my symptoms. I then took a test from celltrend in germany on some of these receptors. I did not get any positive results but I got higher values on M3 receptor which I had my eye on going in to the test. This is responsible for salivation as well as M1.

So if you are like me and have antibodies problem then maybe bethanechol will help HOWEVER I think my problem is very specific so for you a different mechanism could be at play.

Maybe your vagus nerve isn't active enough? Maybe you are low on choline?

I would really like to go to a sunny country and see how much it helps my symptoms however I do not sweat enough and my symptoms are worse when my viens are not vasoconstricted. Im thinking maybe a spray of water would help.

**gastroparesis**

[http://www.webmd.com/digestive-disorders/digestive-disorders-gastroparesis#1](http://www.webmd.com/digestive-disorders/digestive-disorders-gastroparesis#1)

However, the causes of gastroparesis can include:

-   Uncontrolled [diabetes](http://www.webmd.com/diabetes/default.htm)
-   Gastric surgery with injury to the vagus nerve
-   [Medications](http://www.webmd.com/drugs/index-drugs.aspx) such as narcotics and some [antidepressants](http://www.webmd.com/depression/depression-medications-antidepressants)
-   [Parkinson's disease](http://www.webmd.com/parkinsons-disease/default.htm)
-   [Multiple sclerosis](http://www.webmd.com/multiple-sclerosis/default.htm)
-   Rare conditions such as: [amyloidosis](http://www.webmd.com/cancer/lymphoma/amyloidosis-symptoms-causes-treatments) (deposits of protein fibers in tissues and organs) and [scleroderma](http://www.webmd.com/pain-management/scleroderma) (a connective tissue disorder that affects the [skin](http://www.webmd.com/skin-problems-and-treatments/picture-of-the-skin), [blood](http://www.webmd.com/heart/anatomy-picture-of-blood) vessels, skeletal muscles, and internal organs)

I would highlight:
**diabetes
Parkinson's disease
Multiple sclerosis
Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (CFS)
(POTS,** postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome)(specially when cortisol,saliva is low)

because
I am on a outlook of how we are affected by fatty acids.

I suggest to do:
preferably:
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

or using on of the outfits listed here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-3#post-165699](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-3#post-165699)
or
anywhere else
test for:
EPA
DHA
AA
the only requirement is that those tests would have lover and upper limits of their laboratory ranges.
The way I look at results of this tests is that:

1--the ideal value for each of those three fatty acids is in middle of range
2--the acceptable value is plus minus 25% of laboratory range as measured from the middle

It have been observed that in above three neolithic illnesses (and many more)
large group of people would have depressed AA and high EPA.

Not all, but many.
So it is of value to find where one places.

staggering...
I have not invented anything new.

Choline is highly interesting item.

We, all animals and other life, are build of cells.
Cells and content of cells have a boundary.
That boundary are mambranes.
Membranes are double layer of phospholipids.
About **half** of those phopholipids is
Phoshatidyl-**Choline**

Our bodies are able to make Phosphatidyl-Choline from scratch.
It is complicated, but most important it is slow process.
It is even slower when we are sick or old.
Phosphatidyl-Choline that we get from food is digested into individual pieces.

It would be nice to make or take phosphatidyl-choline in laboratory and arrange it in such a way that it would be:
resistant to digestion
able to get in complete unadulterated form
thru gut wall
and other places
near cell membrane
where it could be picked up by the part of membrane that is most missing it

That is already existing and available.

\==================
WE need to spend more time looking it over
discussing it,
figuring better way of making use of it.

Figuring out nomenclatural and legal efforts attempting changing meaning of words in effort to pass imposters for the real McCoy.

I am already taking it.

//
**Nomenclatural** definition, a set or system of names or terms.

I do not know what Mitolipin is.
Found this:
[https://raypeatforum.com/community/...r-cardiolipin-and-mitochondria-support.10398/](https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/mitolipin-dietary-supplement-for-cardiolipin-and-mitochondria-support.10398/)

MitoLipin containing saturated PC, more specifically
dipalmitoylphosphatidylcholine (DPPC) and
distearoylphosphatidylcholine (DSPC).

Servings per container: about 30
Serving size: 40 drops
Each serving contains the following ingredients:

Dipalmitoylphosphatidylcholine (DPPC) - 100mg
Distearoylphosphatidylcholine (DSPC) - 100mg
\----------------------------------------------------

Thanks for sharing.
You said that you didn't notice much of anything after using it.

\================

### JanSz — 2017-03-12T15:50:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluid-and-electrolytes-on-low-carb-diets.10648/post-210187>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=210178)
>
> Yes, I took it for over a month. I'm not sure if you are meant to take it longer for a slower change.

I have not heard of Mitolipin until you told me about it.
The description looks sketchy to me.
Comes via Ray Peat (low regard from Jack)
But that is not the point I am trying to raise.
I am "commited" to BodyBio PC.
That is a product containing phosphatidylCholine and two or three other phospholipids that are common in cell and mitochondrian membranes.
The whole package comes in 4:1 ratio of fatty acids (I assume that it is ratio of LA/ALA but not sure)
They come with rather involved discussion.
They are supposed to go from mouth to cells. Resistant to digestion because of their construction. All in form of miceles. When near to cell membrane the membrane have easy pickings of whatever its needs are.

I knew about them years ago. Long before I heard about @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/).
I was still edgy about them.
But when Jack showed up I started asking him many times about fatty acids.
More or less Jack refereed me to Patricia Kane at some point.
He thinks that he can do much better than she does but she is not bad at all.
That raised her status in my eyes.
Patricia Kane comes with lots of discussions and explanations at the level that talk to me.
I am using her's PC and analyzing and manipulating my fatty acids and the head & neck of phospholipids, with higher diligence now then ever before.
Now comes my point.
I do not expect those modifications to make 16yo man out of my 77yo body.
So,
even if one assumes that Mitolipin is as good or even much better than PC,
all we can expect is to feel normal.
Not superhuman, just normal.

....
This is much different approach than that of sport trainers and bodybuilders, they are talking about starting with very good body and making it to superhuman.
And when they are successful,

they are trying to sell their wares to sick or old

that is different discussion
.....

Every day before I die I want to do 5 key things each day to improve. Lead, grow, create, excel, and serve. I try to become the change I want to see in my world. I become radioactive to the old paradigms. I try to grow myself and my business in some small way to achieve my potential. Potential is worthless without the action of kinetic energy. Daily creation of world-class leadership ideas in some way: resources, experiences, ideas. Chase excellence in all I do. I may not always be successful but I won't allow stasis to hold my mission back. I want to serve my members, patients, clients, colleagues, and community. For too long I used to serve only myself. Action precedes buy-in; it does not follow it. Are you ready?

# Thread: brain fog in the afternoon?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-in-the-afternoon.10649/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12

### campersurfer — 2014-04-25T04:11:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-in-the-afternoon.10649/post-123374>

Hi everyone --
I'm new to this forum (I haven't even gotten my labs done yet as my health insurance doesn't kick in for a few more months ...) but I have a question:
I'm under a lot of pressure at work (big deadline) -- and I'm experiencing brain fog in the afternoon. It seems to clear around 6 pm. I'm also experiencing insomnia at night, then sleeping too long in the morning. Does anyone know what this is? I follow the Bulletproof Exec diet, take a ton of supplements, try to track everything (to see if there are correlations ...) and can't figure out what's causing this.
Thanks so much for your help.

### Lahelada — 2014-05-05T05:20:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-in-the-afternoon.10649/post-125223>

If you are under stress you probably do not have much time to read. Do read however tge LeptinRX as from the little you say I do glean a circadian rythm disturbance.

### nonchalant — 2014-05-05T05:28:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-in-the-afternoon.10649/post-125226>

I agree with Lahelada. Do you eat late at night?
I'm not familiar with the Bulletproof Exec diet, but hopefully it doesn't contain many carbs. Your afternoon sleepiness sounds like my SAD days.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-05T06:06:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-in-the-afternoon.10649/post-125229>

big deadline = excessive cortisol = unwound collagen = poor energy efficiency.

### campersurfer — 2014-05-05T06:38:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-in-the-afternoon.10649/post-125232>

Hi everybody --
Thank you so much for responding. I did a bunch of reading on jackkruse.com after posting that -- and have self-diagnosed as having the circadian imbalance that Dr. K talks about in Adrenal Fatigue Rx.
However -- I think I also have some kind of gut issue which may be leading to my cortisol and circadian rhythms being out of whack (not to mention now being late on my work deadline, and now totally unable to focus due to severe insomnia and sleep-deprivation, which is compounding the problem and turning it into a Catch-22).
So for now I'm trying to focus on bringing down my cortisol at night and following the other circadian protocols (I'm wearing my blue blocker glasses right now!) and healing my gut. Sound right to you?
Anything else you recommend while I'm in this state of "emergency"?
Thank you so much for the help!

### campersurfer — 2014-05-05T06:46:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-in-the-afternoon.10649/post-125233>

One more thing to add -- I've been doing the Bulletproof Exec diet with Intermittent Fasting for at least a year. But I only recently found out that IF is maybe not so good for women? I'm afraid the IF coupled with the big work deadline might have pushed me into adrenal fatigue.
It is low carb (though it does include vegetables) -- but with the last two weeks of severe insomnia, I have noticed that eating carbs (white rice) seems to help my insomnia a bit. Meaning: on a day where the only carbs I ate were vegetables, I might struggle to fall asleep till 4 am then wake at 9 am. On a day where I ate some white rice, I might fall asleep at 12, wake at 2, fall asleep again at 3:30, wake at 6, fall asleep again at 7, wake at 9. (For reference: until very recently I was regularly sleeping 8-10 hours a night).
I'm trying the Leptin Rx too now. (I'm desperate to fix this!) Thank you so much again for y'all's help and advice.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-05T06:57:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-in-the-afternoon.10649/post-125235>

Tonight's Q & A last question was ironically on this very issue...........quantum biology at work yet again.

# Thread: Beef Liver Pâté in Jacks cook book Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beef-liver-p%C3%A2t%C3%A9-in-jacks-cook-book-question.10650/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kyrakitty — 2014-04-25T05:15:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beef-liver-p%C3%A2t%C3%A9-in-jacks-cook-book-question.10650/post-123378>

So, forgive the dumb question but I really want to make this. Do I cook the liver first or is the liver supposed to be raw and only warmed up? Thanks to whomever is kind enough to answer ))

Ingredients
• 3/4 cup ghee
• 1/2 cup onion, chopped
• 1 lb liver (beef, chicken or goose)
• 1 1/2 tsp cajun spice
• 1/8 tsp black pepper
• 1 tsp lemon juice
• 1 hard boiled egg
• 1/8 tsp cayenne pepper

Directions:
Melt 1/2 ghee and onions in pan until onions are soft for about 5 mins. Remove from heat. Add all other ingredients, stir until ghee is melted. Put mixture in a blender with egg. Blend at high speed untilpureed. Take out of blender and shape into a ball or log. Place in fridge until cool about 1 or 2 hrs.

### Inger — 2014-04-25T08:13:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beef-liver-p%C3%A2t%C3%A9-in-jacks-cook-book-question.10650/post-123385>

It must be cooked liver - with raw it would end up as a bloody mess

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-25T08:35:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beef-liver-p%C3%A2t%C3%A9-in-jacks-cook-book-question.10650/post-123388>

Thx Inger )) you're the best!

### Inger — 2014-04-25T11:06:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beef-liver-p%C3%A2t%C3%A9-in-jacks-cook-book-question.10650/post-123411>

Kitty it is my pleasure
i wish I could do more! I am not a geek  and when I do can answer/know the answer or can help it makes me happy

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-cant-i-sleep-past-3-30.10651/post-123386>

Hello. Any suggestions will be gratefully received!

I now have black out blinds.
I'm avoiding fake lights.
No snacks ( for a few days).

I'm so sensitive to light, noise, vibration (when someone is walking around it makes me jolt awake), computers, chemicals, foods, other people, the planets. ....... the list goes on

It seems to be worse second half of my cycle.

I start going back to sleep when the sun has come up fully (about 6am) but then I get woken up by my daughter, the dog, partner.......

Sometimes I sleep really well and I'm going a bit mad trying to work out why.

ps I have trouble with adrenals etc

> [lubylouUK said:](/goto/post?id=126612)
>
> How can you tell by my post code??

hi Lubylou

It just means your location, and location is important. If your post code is in a location with lots of non native emfs (built up areas, cities etc etc) you are likely to have more problems with sleep, autophagy and electron steal than if your post code puts you in a location with much less non native emf (rural for example). It means where you live matters, it may help answer why you are still having problems despite what you are already doing, and that may help you look for solutions ......
