# Thread: "Optimal" East Coast Vacation Spot?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-east-coast-vacation-spot.10721/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2014-05-02T15:54:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-east-coast-vacation-spot.10721/post-124558>

My DH has said he doesn’t think the plane ride to New Orleans would be a good idea for me. I tend to agree. He thinks I should spend a week in a pine forest in the Poconos. I’m considering this. I’m also considering trying to find something secluded at a beach where I can get fresh seafood, ground daily in the ocean and get some sunshine. I won’t be able to have any of those at the Poconos. The only good thing about that is I’d be much closer to home in case something were to happen with my health being so frail right now.

But, ultimately I think the beach is my best option right now. We want to find something that is in driving distance – 12 hours max, really. Most commercialized/tourist beaches now have wifi and are so congested they are not going to be low EMF areas. Does anyone know of any secluded beaches or homes that are secluded with easy access to a beach along the east coast? This is what I’m considering my last ditch effort. I need to try something to mix things up and see how I respond. I hate to leave my family, but desperate times call for desperate measures. I want to try just a week or two to see what kind of effect this has on my health.

### Josh — 2014-05-02T19:14:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-east-coast-vacation-spot.10721/post-124650>

MDI has less Lyme and there are 50+ miles of carriage paths built by the Rockefellers to walk on plus many other trails without much brush. I am not sure how much Lyme's there is on the island. A lot of the park and coast is exposed rock period.

### BJK77 — 2014-05-02T19:21:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-east-coast-vacation-spot.10721/post-124652>

That's awesome to hear Josh. I never really researched it, but had been told by many friends that Maine (and obviously CT) had very high Lyme rates. I can't remember the name of the town we stayed in last summer, but it was very close to MDI. Fresh lobster daily was amazing! I kept thinking I needed to head south for sun, but right now too much sun is not good for me and I'm sure I can get just enough up north this time of year anyway. Maine is sounding better and better.

### Josh — 2014-05-02T20:15:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-east-coast-vacation-spot.10721/post-124663>

Get a place with a good fireplace or stove where you can sit in front of an open fire naked for long periods. WHen the sun is too much, IR from a good fire is often quite nourishing. Get cold in the ocean and do IR from fire while eating seafood and drinking water. I like the "quiet" side of MDI over around Southwest Harbor. It may be early to get a rental, but some people open their houses around now.

[http://www.mdivacationhomes.com/](http://www.mdivacationhomes.com/)

# Thread: Misty Williams Tribute
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-05-03T02:59:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124806>

Sad news that Misty has departed. She was a loyal long term member of this community so I wanted to honor her enormous contributions over the years.

Highlights for me were:

Implementing the new forum in a few weeks from initial suggestion
The Optimal reset programme
Facilitating the monthly webinars and Q and A's
Supporting others in their journey
Her passion and humor

So thanks Misty and her team you will be greatly missed.

I have worked in a few companies and always get sad when colleagues you know and respect leave but I have always deplored the way that their name and achievements become unmentionable after they leave. This is a great community let's not fall into such behaviors.

> Last month, I notified Dr. Kruse that I will not be continuing on as the business manager for JackKruse.com after today (April 30).
>
> It has been my pleasure to support Dr. Kruse and all of you on your journeys to optimal. This has been more than a project for me … I have been on this journey with you. I’ve found a lot of joy in finding ways to bring in the right kind of support to help us along as we're decoding our health challenges and leveraging the incredible information Dr. Kruse is sharing with us. I’ve been moved by your stories and inspired by your resolve to change your lives!
>
> I’m especially grateful for the hard work my team has put in to improving this community and supporting each one of you. They’ve been champs.
>
> Thank you all for the love and gratitude and patience you’ve shown as we’ve all grown together. I will miss you!
>
> Misty

### Lahelada — 2014-05-03T03:08:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124807>

Glad you said something Paul.I did not know her but she deserves our well wishes for her pastures new .
Misty, good luck!

### Lava — 2014-05-03T03:17:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124812>

### cantweight — 2014-05-03T03:18:42+0300

Thanks lady!!!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124824>

Hopefully, she will stop in once in a while and let us know how she is doing when she is ready. Misty was always so fun and so very very helpful.

### Nick — 2014-05-03T04:25:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124826>

Seeing Misty and Jack sipping from wine glasses, having lots of laughs, in some of the early webinar Q&A's will always be fondly remembered.

Thank you Misty for helping the journey begin for the community.

### tellmisty — 2014-05-03T20:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124903>

You guys make me teary.

Thank you so much ... I appreciate and respect each one of you, from the bottom of my heart. This journey is HARD. I know firsthand the dedication and commitment it takes to delve in and _sustain_ our journeys to optimal. I've heard from so many of you who have been beaten down and have felt hopeless as you try to get well, and it has been my honor to support you and to create space for all of us to get better together applying the revolutionary things Dr. Kruse has been teaching us over the last 3 years.

I am glad that you've noticed what we have worked so hard to do for you ... for all of us. It means so much; to my team, too. They are rock stars ... they really are. I don't even \*know\* of another team that does what we do. I really hope Dr. Kruse is able to find a great team to take things over. My team will continue to support Sandy (his wife), as needed, as she manages things until they have another team in place.

My next journey is packing up and moving to Austin ... I appreciate your good vibes; this is going to be a crazy month!

Namaste, my friends. xo

### Josh — 2014-05-03T20:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124904>

Keep the faith.....thank you for all your efforts.

Great to hear from you, Misty. Welcome to Texas! Don't be a stranger!

### Lahelada — 2014-05-07T21:09:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intl-emf-research.10736/post-125677>

BAFFLED BIRDS...
[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/...ic-robins-henrik-mouritsen-science-broadband/](http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2014/05/140507-birds-migration-electromagnetic-robins-henrik-mouritsen-science-broadband/)

### Gagnrad — 2014-05-07T21:31:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intl-emf-research.10736/post-125679>

The birds and the bees …

And bees?

> Early experiments revealed that bee colonies placed near electric fields became hyperactive. The bees, “in a strong electric field became aggressive, stinging each other to death.” They stopped producing brood and destroyed their own young. These bees would either completely abandon their hives or instead barricade every opening so none could enter or leave.
>
> Recent studies have found a correlation between colony collapse disorder and the EMF from cellular and wireless technology. A study was conducted where test honeybee colonies were subjected to cellular frequencies for 10 minutes daily. After only ten days of cellular EMF exposure, no worker bees returned to their hives and simply went missing. ...

[http://www.emfs.com/article/are-emfs-responsible-for-vanishing-bees](http://www.emfs.com/article/are-emfs-responsible-for-vanishing-bees)

That's from a site that sells EMF meters.

On the other hand, there's this:

> … there does not seem to be evidence of EMFs or power lines adversely affecting bees. Indeed, one study (abstract below) found that the strip of land along power lines in the USA, because it is protected from development, can be particularly attractive to bees.

[http://www.emfs.info/The+Science/Agriculture/bees/](http://www.emfs.info/The+Science/Agriculture/bees/)

That's from the UK National Grid.

Everyone has an interest ... but I guess in the end the truth will out. Of course, since bees are necessary for the pollination of crops, if they are being wantonly harmed we'll all be in trouble.

### Lahelada — 2014-05-07T22:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intl-emf-research.10736/post-125683>

### Clayton — 2014-05-03T17:52:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faraday-cage-magnetico.10737/post-124892>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=124861)
>
> Thanks to @[Jude](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/904/) mentioning a Faraday cage a few times, DH just went and ordered me one. I'm wondering though if there would be any problem using it with a Magnetico? Of course I'll call and ask Dr. Bonlie as well, but I wanted to see what some of you thought.

I just had a phone consult with Michael Neuert this week. In regards to a faraday cage make sure it is one with material that you can ground. Faraday cages with cotton coating around the silver wiring are useless for grounding (although they block out Radio frequencies well), and this will be bad news for your body voltage. They are probably ok if you can cut your breakers at night but where I am living I don't have access to my breakers and therefore have to ground my faraday cage. He gave me a website selling good cages that you can ground.

[http://rfsensitivesolutions.com/canopies.php](http://rfsensitivesolutions.com/canopies.php)

He also have me a few cheap options for building your own faraday cage. I would say before you spend big money on some remediation it would definitely be worth your while to consult with him beforehand, it was definitely worthwhile. Even better have meters in your possession when you make the call and make up a body voltage meter as per instructions on his site.

Hello Everyone,

I am new.

I have concerns about the positive field emanating from the Magnetico Sleep pad (smaller living quarters, where adjacent living spaces (eg, another bedroom or sitting area of the living room) are within the positive field). As I understand matters from Dr. Bonalie, the perimeter of the bed emanates a positive field 45 degree above and 45 degrees below the magnet(s). The perimeter's positive field range relates to the width of the bed at 10 gauss and is 1/2 of the width (i.e. 1/2 of a double bed size (54") is 27"), yet, this changes to 40" for 20 gauss (for a double sized bed).

Dr. Bonalie suggests use of galvanized steel on the walls, which left me a little bewildered (but this often happens to me). I tried to contact some EMF sources to find out what might be suggested within their discipline's frame of reference and only one, during a very brief conversation, remarked that a blocking medium close to the magnets could destroy the magnets (which are permanent, fixed magnets); the others I contacted provided what I understand are typical emf mitigating materials that could be placed around the perimeter of the bed (although they were informed the positive field problem came from permanent magnets).

It must be that permanent magnets involve different mediation remedies. Although coming from a different direction, the problem is the same. Can typical emf solutions be detrimental to permanent magnets, if placed too close them?

I would suspect that someone, perhaps many, perhaps Mr. Neuret, may like clarify this important matter, which is two-fold (if you will allow a perspective of concern to me):

Can material like the Faraday mosquito net detrimentally affect the magnets of the Magnetico Sleep pad if placed too close (touches)?

Is there a remedy to mitigate the range of the positive field that emits from the Magnetico Sleep pad. Apart from close living quarters, there is also the issue of children or animals staying for long periods in the range of the positive field.

Regards to you all.

In closing I just realized that this forum is a year old. I am hoping someone may answer.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-15T02:43:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faraday-cage-magnetico.10737/post-164315>

Your Zip code determines you circadian cycles. Your zip code is more important than your genetic code. Bad zip code = poor circadian cycles = poor autophagy = poor sleep = electron steal syndrome = calcium efflux (EE4) = water can not stay bound to proteins with negative charges = why humans are all dehydrated despite their actions to drink water = all diseases. And chronobiology, seasonal and circadian rhythms, are the main "glue" that holds together all the various ecosystems and biochemical processes, to each other, and also to the planets and to the universe itself. ### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-04T15:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-glow.10742/post-124978>

**Clarke's Three Laws** are three ["laws"](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adage) of prediction They are:

1.  When a distinguished but elderly scientist states that something is possible, he is almost certainly right. When he states that something is impossible, he is very probably wrong.
2.  The only way of discovering the limits of the possible is to venture a little way past them into the impossible.
3.  Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic.

My rule: Humans will keep finding more about themselves as the learn how to harness subatomic particles for technologic breakthroughs.
You must have optimism for the unknown. To get there you must be willing to erase the known.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-glow.10742/post-125141>

Had a meeting today...

Did some work, looking at posture and gait, and whohaa...

Something out of this world walked by, stopped, listened to my thoughts and ideas... She wanted me to analyze her, so i did.

My friends at work said it was almost a moment of pain for them, you know when a couple does a little to much on the dancefloor.

We where so lifted for a moment that time stood still, her smell, her arms and the neck... Just these details that give you hope.

So, I am a dream hacker. I mine my dreams for gold daily. I have been dreaming a lot lately which Jack says is a good bio marker of sleep quality. So, that great! But the real benefit is my dreams supplement my reading on the blog and sometimes help highlight key points I may have not given enough attention to.

I have been reading the latest blog a lot. Then, last night I had a dream in which cholesterol was being highlighted. I got the impression that the real magic or highlight of the blog was in the part around cholesterol. So, I read that part again and again and was looking for a bit of additional magic that I may have missed. What was the message of my dream? Why was cholesterol a big highlight in the blog my dreams were pointing to?

When I think of cholesterol now, I think about conductivity. I think how in the end, an optimized system has optimal conductivity and in the end this may be the key biomarker of optimal health that we're shooting for.

Conductivity must reflect every aspect of the system. Do you think this is what my dream was getting at?

### ssj3 — 2014-05-04T11:30:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.10744/post-124959>

Two things I can think of are:
1- File was corrupted when you downloaded it from 23andMe and you will need to re-download it.
2- From memory I think 23andMe data is downloaded as a zip file. If it is a zip file I think you will need to extract the text file from the archive and use that with genetic genie.

# Thread: Yikes! Med mix up.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yikes-med-mix-up.10745/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2014-05-04T16:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yikes-med-mix-up.10745/post-124998>

I am a 53 yr old male with T2D/Hashi's. So through some prescription error and me not checking labels I took what amounts to a double dose of Synthroid for six weeks. I am supposed to be taking 112 mcg Synthroid and 10mcg Cytomel. Cytomel scrip needed renewal my Doc's nurse ordered Synthroid instead of Cytomel. Scrip arrived in mail and I didn't check label. 50mcg Synthroid tabs look like 5mcg Cytomel. Thinking I was taking 10 mcg Cytomel I was taking 100 mcg Synthroid on top of my 112 mcg Synthroid for six weeks.
Thinking back I realized there were some symptoms like hot flashes, but the prominent effect was BG which has increased to it's highest level since going paleo 3 years ago. No heart palps though. I have also developed torturous acid reflux which massive amounts of betaine has done little to alleviate.

Endo said to lay off Synthroid for 3 days and resume. I have not taken it for 12 days now and except for bad heartburn every time I eat I feel okay and BG has come down some. Any thoughts?

### Shijin13 — 2014-05-04T16:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yikes-med-mix-up.10745/post-124999>

If BG has come backdown - I'd say you're ok to go back to your normal synthroid... though you might want to consider running thyroid labs to see where you're at before adding back in...

I'd also ask for the correct refill of cytomel.. and add them back in together....

I've also noticed that when my thyroid meds are too high or too low, my FBG goes nuts! I think its a great indicator to help thyroid patients zero in on the perfect dose for their meds...

A quick story: The last time I was in Orlando was to meet my Acupuncture teacher and a Japanese man who called himself a "shinto priest". This was 20 years ago or so. After Orlando, the Japanese man and I went on the road to visit people around Florida that this Japanese man was doing healing with. We went to Gainsville, where he met a woman about your age in a motel. He explained that her family was all at her house, so she wanted to meet there. His typical session was 45 minutes. I sat outside for 3 hours until they both came out glowing...I asked what had happened and he said "sometimes with the right person, all god and angels come down to help and we both get blessed." I am sure some would read this and come to "assumptions" having known him well, I doubt that was what happened...So I said well "what about me?" He replied, clients such as this woman give me the energy to work on people like you....I have lightened up a little since then.....Bon Voyage....Yi Lu Ping An....

I would hate to see you waste too much of your time and resources on all that. I have only recently started to read this Forum but honesty it is way too much to keep up with and have pretty much gone back to the blogs and webinars.

Technology can most definitly be a 'trap' Your information is so valuabe and we are GETTING it as it stands so why get overly stressed about it. ### Josh — 2014-05-06T20:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/may-4-2014-webinar-q-a-alert.10751/post-125526>

Can you say "electron transplant..."

[http://www.bbc.com/news/health-27282832](http://www.bbc.com/news/health-27282832)

> Injecting the blood of young mice into older rodents boosted their brainpower, a study found.
>
> Scientists at Stanford University plan to carry out trials in people in the hope that new treatments for dementia can be developed.
>
> A UK dementia research charity said the human significance was unknown.
>
> [Continue reading the main story](http://www.bbc.com/news/health-27282832#story_continues_2)
> **“Start Quote**
> There are factors present in blood from young mice that can recharge an old mouse's brain so that it functions more like a younger one”
>
> Dr Tony Wyss-Coray Stanford University School of Medicine
> In the study, published in [Nature Medicine](http://www.nature.com/nm/index.html), mice aged 18 months were given injections of the fluid part of blood (plasma) taken from mice aged three months.
>
> The injected mice performed better on memory tests than mice of the same age that had not been given blood plasma.
>
> "There are factors present in blood from young mice that can recharge an old mouse's brain so that it functions more like a younger one," said Dr Tony Wyss-Coray of Stanford University School of Medicine.
>
> "We're working intensively to find out what those factors might be and from exactly which tissues they originate."
>
> He said it was not known whether the same was true in humans, but a clinical trial was planned.

> _· [6 days ago](http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-1-gutcollagen-link/#IDComment824314841)_
>
> Very interesting blog here, Dr. Kruse. I've been reading quite a few of your posts as well as some threads on your forum. I have been counseling so many people lately who are stricken by biotoxin illnesses - mold, lyme, parasites, etc. I'm curious, do you believe these folks are suffering from biotoxins because they have electron steal syndrome? If they replace the lost electrons they wouldn't be affected by the biotoxins?
> [jackkruse](http://jackkruse.com/)_· [6 days ago](http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-1-gutcollagen-link/#IDComment824585285)_
>
> **_Every disease out there is from electron steal syndrome. It just depends where the electrons are being lost. There are only two change programs in mitochondria. Autophagy and apoptosis. 1\. Excess uvb due to ozone hole causes **[hydrogen bonding](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/hbond.html) changes** in water vapour held in clouds. This means when it rains in Australia the water is less able to bond to proteins such as collagen and other hydrophylic molecules and more likely that such weakened bonds to become disrupted by man made non native EMFs and less ability to charge seperate and form EZ layers of sufficiernt depth which leads to dehydration and less electron and proton energy transduction and less ability to fight oxidative stress leading to disease states.

2\. The upper atmosphere below the ozone hole contains higher concentrations of halides such as flourine, chlorine and bromine due to industrial waste processes. These halides end up in rainwater and when ingested by us via food or drink blocks semiconduction so that iodine is not absorbed and we become further dehydrated further causing us to lose salts.

# Thread: Myopia, optics, epigenetics, and brainpower
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-optics-epigenetics-and-brainpower.10757/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NeilBB — 2014-05-05T22:06:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-optics-epigenetics-and-brainpower.10757/post-125338>

Random thought experiment on myopia, optics, epigenetics, and brainpower. Your answers to Rob about glasses and contacts started my mind wandering on a tangent again…

It didn't at first make much sense to me that contacts would be worse than glasses, especially since the eyeball already naturally contains two focusing lenses (lens and cornea) that are already very similar to contact lenses… But in a myopic person, using glasses instead of contacts does allow more unfocused (scattered) light to enter the eye in peripheral vision. Contacts eliminate this completely. So, could more of this “scattered light” be actually a good thing for the retina? And if so, could even myopia itself then be some kind of epigenetic quantum adaptation to increase power to the retina and eventually cerebral cortex in the holographic brain???
I looked it up and there is stuff published on myopia and evolution, and it possibly being an adaptive trait, but it’s usually something along the lines of, “it’s adaptive cause those half blind people weren’t fit enough to go kill a bear, so they were more likely to survive because they were sheltered.” Maybe… Myopia is also associated with insulin resistance and dysfunctional collagen also. Loren Cordain has studied some of this. Haven’t had time to read it all yet.

But here’s an interesting thought… What if this more “scattered and unfocused light of myopia” somehow increases “optical diversity” to the retina in some way that actually enhances the optic power system of the brain? Like a strange pseudo-prism QED effect of sorts. If so, it could potentially explain why “myopics” are typically “smarter,”e.g. why “nerds wear glasses.” Not because they stick their head in books and it weakens their eyes, as is commonly thought. But perhaps because the myopia itself supercharges their optically powered brain by providing a less sharp, but more neuro-optically powerful experience of light? So, maybe they are smarter to begin with and that’s why they are always reading…

### Shijin13 — 2014-05-05T22:20:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-optics-epigenetics-and-brainpower.10757/post-125339>

Interesting Neil...

so if someone had extremely myopic vision say 700/20 and 750/20 and got lasik... how does that impact the optical diversity?

### nonchalant — 2014-05-05T22:26:31+0300

### SeaHorse — 2014-05-05T22:55:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-optics-epigenetics-and-brainpower.10757/post-125345>

I have had this huge book of William Bates' articles sitting on my shelf for years…he was an ophthalmologist in the 1920s who believed many vision issues were a function of improper use of muscles. He is considered a quack for the most part which makes him intriguing.
I started leafing through it the other day. One of the "exercises" to improve eye function is to let the sun shine on your closed lids. The enemy of good eyesight is hard focus and "fixing." Anyway…..as a totally-nearsighted-legally-blind-without- correction person, I'm all for better optics in the brain and eyes.

I've chosen to wear glasses this past year after 40 plus years of contacts, interesting.

Will be very curious about where this discussion leads us.

My gut feeling is that lasik is a mistake…changes the form not the functioning. Doesn't everything just start all over again?

### NeilBB — 2014-05-05T22:58:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-optics-epigenetics-and-brainpower.10757/post-125346>

Lasik partially vaporizes and reshapes the cornea to focus light farther back in the myopic’s eyeball, to reach retina since eyeball is elongated. It does not add an extra layer of anything on top of the cornea like a contact does, but it certainly does change the myopic’s optics. So whether it is a problem or not depends on why the contacts are a problem in first place… The important question is, “is myopia an adaption that enhances the power of the brain in some way??” I understand enough about QED and quantum brain theory only to offer the hypothesis, I don’t know the answer.

# Thread: Educating non-doctors

### nonchalant — 2014-05-06T02:28:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-non-doctors.10758/post-125374>

If you were to be asked by a friend where they should start in getting healthy, where do you begin?
Do you inundate them with all kinds of information about water, sunshine, magnetism, seafood, avoiding artificial light at night, eating supper early, no msg, no grains, etc etc etc?
Do you suggest one thing, like avoiding wheat, and see how they do on that, and then go to step #2?

If you inundate them, do they run away screaming? If you go the slow path, do they get bored and revert back to their previous habits? How about Jack's method, educating them in the areas they are curious?

If you suggest they get more sunshine, their veggie-oil laden skin burns quickly. If you suggest CT, their veggie-oil laden skin freezes. If you suggest RO water, they might give up sodas for a while, but probably don't notice much difference from tap water. Suggest raw seafood, and watch their expressions of shock?

Perhaps we need to tailor the information based on their needs and circumstances. Get your foot in the door by suggesting a supplement or two, and go from there?
Should we just suggest they talk to Audrey?

### caroline — 2014-05-06T06:31:35+0300

everyone is such a smart ass around here today ......

I think this is such a tough question ..... everyone may like how we are looking and how healthy we are ...but they need to want to do the work ...just like we did and are doing.....

oysters would be such a great way to start.

Over the last couple of days I spoke at length with two young men who seem to know intuitively the right things to do - they both blew me away.

One is 44 and the other 19 ........they both are very bright and want a competitive edge - but also laid back and cool and loving life. Brian , the older guy - didn't like tech stuff at all - his phone was a really old one and he wasn't even sure where it was!

neither ate much if any bread or pasta ... they like salads and protein. They didn't have a clue what Paleo is.

Also both were very family orientated. None of their friends did the same things. Their friends ate and drank to excess and were overweight and unhealthy.

They hike and other outdoor stuff and sports and really valued being healthy so they could do the things they want to do.

They seem to learn lessons from their parents and grandparents .......and really think about their health intuitively.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-24T03:23:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-non-doctors.10758/post-135358>

well, i have no discipline. i never have. but, if i feel ill then i just cant stand it and i will try things to feel better. thats my motivation- i dont like feeling bad.

with advice for others, i would never give it unless they specifically asked. theres a lady who works at the shop who i really like, i would almost call her a friend. anyway last month she was on this awful 600 calorie diet. its mostly soup and those little bar things that they sell. just the worst diet you could imagine. she was also exercising every day. she must have been starving and i couldnt understand how she was doing it.

well, i really wanted to say something. just the amount of discomfort that she didnt need to be in. the weight that would come piling back on. the health thing. but- i said nothing. i would ask her how she was getting on, she would tell me and i would nod.

its her life. its not my business to interfere. she wasnt asking for advice. she just wanted to share her experience. it drives me nuts when you are trying to talk to someone and they are like "have you tried yoga/ vitamin c/ kettlebells ?" i know they are trying to help, but it just makes me feel that they dont trust me to sort out my own problems. and also, maybe im not ready for yoga, dont understand it, dont know how it would relate to my life etc.

the only time i would ever give advice is if they are asking for it directly. and even then i would be quite vague. i might say something like "well, im having some success with eating more seafood". only then if they were curious would i give them a link to a website or recommend a book. let them find out for themselves. no-one likes it when someone is proclaiming from on high that they must now give up watching tv and sit in a cold bath every day, and quickly before they die of cancer. let them find out at their own pace and apply things to their life that seem relevant to them.

im not the worlds expert on this subject, but ive noticed in my own life people getting into one thing or another and before you know it they are dispensing free advice every second of the day. i have an old friend who, when i was using facebook, would continually post those motivational posters because she is into this mad new age thing. any problem i ever posted about, she would pipe up with some free advice about this bloody new age philosophy that i have no interest in. she must feel great to be the guru, but it really did my head in, and i never talk to her now. if anything it drove me away from the thing she was trying to promote.

# Thread: Help me to optimize sleep (sleep tips)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-to-optimize-sleep-sleep-tips.10763/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-05-06T11:45:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-to-optimize-sleep-sleep-tips.10763/post-125442>

I am looking to optimize my sleep and also to give actionable advice to family members. Anyone got any tips that are not below:

-   Read Sleep related blogs at [http://jackkruse.com/?s=sleep](http://jackkruse.com/?s=sleep)
-   Review information at [http://www.helpguide.org/life/sleep\_tips.htm](http://www.helpguide.org/life/sleep_tips.htm)
-   Bedroom is completely dark using blackout shades or eye masks, preferably cold, comfortable
-   Lights out in bedroom no later than 10.30pm and get 7-9hrs sleep (Weekdays and Weekends)
-   Watch the sun set and rise each day
-   Get at least 30 minutes of light by going outside in the morning before doing anything else
-   At night turn on lamps with red/amber bulbs
-   Avoid blue light after sunset such as use of TVs, computers and bright lighting
-   Install filters such as [f.lux](https://justgetflux.com/) for the PC/Mac/IPad/Iphone and [Bluelight Filter for Eye Care](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=jp.ne.hardyinfinity.bluelightfilter.free) for Android
-   Buy blue blocker sunglasses and wear during the evening
-   No gadgets in bedroom at night such as electric alarm clocks. Wake naturally with the sun rise.
-   Reduce stress levels through meditation, exercise, hobbies, sports, music etc
-   Get an electrician to install a switch in the master bedroom that turns off electricity in all the bedrooms
-   Make sure there is no wifi in your environment
-   If you have a smart meter take steps to mitigate this risk
-   Consider buying a Magnetico Sleep Pad. See [https://magneticosleep.com/](https://magneticosleep.com/)
-   Buy an earthing sheet that can be connected to the earth/ground outside
-   Walk barefoot on the earth 50 minutes before sleep
-   Exercise ideally at 4-6pm but no later than 7pm as this can raise cortisol
-   Take magnesium malate around 6pm and magnesium orotate 1hr before bed (150-350mg)
-   Avoid all grains and dairy, stimulants, tea, coffee, alcohol and heavy metals
-   No food 4 hours prior to sleep e.g. no snacking
-   If you need to go to the bathroom at night wear a [red led headlamp](http://fasttechcdn.com/products/105/1053800/1053800-1.jpg) or use torch with amber bulb
-   Ensure there is no dirty electricity in the bedroom by installing dirty electricity filters
-   Remove all electronics from bedroom not just turn them off as it generates ambient electricity
-   Sex before bed and in the morning will release oxytocin. See [health benefits of sex](http://www.sydneytherapist.com/health-benefits-of-sex.html)
-   Determine if you are Leptin resistant. See [Jack Kruse Leptin Series](http://jackkruse.com/table-of-contents/#series-leptin) and follow protocols such as Big Arse Breakfasts (BABs)
-   Don't wear a watch to bed
-   Lab Tests that are relevant for sleep: 24hr Adrenal Stress Index, Melatonin, DHEA, Progesterone
-   Avoid anti-depressants and pain medications
-   If you are chronically tired get a sleep study/test for sleep apnea
-   If sleep not resolving from these recommendations then consider the following supplements: 1hr prior to sleep: l-theanine 100-200mg, 600mg NAC, r-Lipoic Acid, high dose coQ10 ubiquinol

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-18T16:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-to-optimize-sleep-sleep-tips.10763/post-144185>

Light sets your bodies many circadian rhythms. Not sleep.

Our ancestors could have healthy multi-phasic sleep because they had no light at night.

We have no night because of light.

### Penny — 2014-10-23T06:55:41+0300

### Da-mo — 2014-10-23T09:21:47+0300

### Lahelada — 2014-10-23T22:12:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-to-optimize-sleep-sleep-tips.10763/post-144880>

On the same note as Da-mo: keep cold water and a wash cloth beside your bed. If you wake up you are more than likely too hot. Wipe yourself with the damp cloth and let evaporate. By the time you are dry you are probably also asleep.

# Thread: summer beetroot brownies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kovita — 2014-05-06T12:33:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125449>

this is not strictly epi-paleo recipe, but the period of carbs is coming with the long light cycles and these brownies are great and there are no nuts in them. I hope you will love it!
you need:

-   2 and half cups of grated beetroot
-   1 cup of honey
-   4 eggs
-   1/2 cup of coconut oil
-   a dash of salt

-   1/2 cup of raw cocoa powder
-   1/2 teaspoon of baking soda but works wihtout it
-   optionally handfull of raw cocoa nibs or freshly grated coconut

Cook the beetroot till it is soft, get rid of excess water and add the honey. in a blender blend together the beetroot with honey and all other ingredients till it has paste like consistency. Add cocoa, cocoa nibs or fresh coconut and stir well. Pour to the baking form over the baking paper. Bake for 40 minuts at 170 degrees Celsius. The brownies will be dense and wet so keep it in the fridge. Sometimes it has beefy colour,sometimes it is pinkish and sometimes more brownish depending on the beetroot quality and the amount of cocoa { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### kovita — 2014-05-07T09:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125600>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=125582)
>
> Beets have a lot of sugar too .....

Caroline, this is summer recipe amd honey and beets are not too bad source of sugars. You can use stevia, but I personally dislike the taste. I usually add much less honey and it is still very good. I discovered during the past few weeks my body works better with 50-100g of some carbs per day, using vegetables and something like these brownies for example. My kidney function went to great from poor immediately when I changed to even more fat, low moderate protein and this amounts of carbs per day. I have A blood group and i never was really protein person, so it is confirmed now I do better on lower protein  It seems it may be helpfull for my serotonin in the toilet as well. As said many times each person is unique and diet is not static and must change as our body needs change.

### caroline — 2014-05-07T11:00:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125607>

I totally agree Kovita .... I only made the comment because some might not stop to think how much sugar is in beets.
It would not work for me - everyone needs to pay attention and figure out their own n=1.

I am so glad you are feeling better - I was worried about you.

High fat is so much better for me too ..... and low protein....

we need to pay attention to everything...

# Thread: Self Respect

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sooperb — 2014-05-06T15:34:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-respect.10766/post-125470>

“Don't be too hard on yourself. There are plenty of people willing to do that for you. Do your best and surrender the rest. Tell yourself, "I am doing the best I can with what I have in this moment. That is all I can ever expect of anyone, including me." Love yourself and be proud of everything you do, even your mistakes, because your mistakes mean you're trying.

If you feel like others are not treating you with love and respect, check your price tag. Perhaps you subconsciously marked yourself down. Because it's YOU who tells others what you're worth by showing them what you are willing to accept for your time and attention. So get off the clearance rack. If you don't value and respect yourself, wholeheartedly, no one else will either.”

― - Unknown

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-06T16:41:40+0300

### Melanie Procter — 2014-05-06T16:37:25+0300

OK Doc,

Let's get real. We have all enjoyed our wine as you do. Do us a big big favor. What do you do when u have a bit of a wineover? I am sure many of us love a classic wine as u do! Help us with the wineover RX! We will be grateful 4ever

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-06T17:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-some-fun.10769/post-125502>

Water.......lots of it. Curcumin, NAC, Mg, resveratrol, followed by 16 hours of IFing and at least 4 hours of CT the following day, limit blue light exposure and use red light under my tongue or in nose.

### Danco3636 — 2014-05-06T18:32:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-some-fun.10769/post-125512>

I had too much to drink one night. Woke up - well a bit hung over.
So the first thing I did was slam some water, CoQ and went out to my very cold pool and sat in it for about 40-45 minutes until I was shivering like crazy (deep CT). Got out warmed up with some light stretching and bam! I was good to go.
Fasted until noon.
Felt so good I even went out for a trail walk/ run later.

### Melanie Procter — 2014-05-07T04:09:24+0300

### Melanie Procter — 2014-05-07T04:16:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-burning-pathways.10775/post-125578>

Hi,

Today a friend of mine who is a very influential fitness trainer was giving a talk. He stated "We can't burn fat because humans don't make the enzymes to burn fat." I thought that it was the craziest thing I had ever heard. He seemed to want to learn the various "fuel burning pathways" and he asked which of these Jack Kruse blogs would give him the deep dive into this subject. Off hand, I thought of the Mitochondria RX, the PPP, and even the podcast when Jack was interviewed by Ben Greenfield (a real gem). Can anyone recommend better ones on this subject or ones to add to this mix? I thought I would go through and summarize some of the more challenging ones for him. I am pretty good at putting some of these blogs in laymen's terms for people who need help understanding them.

Mel

# Thread: Earthpulse
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthpulse.10776/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-05-07T05:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthpulse.10776/post-125587>

Curious to hear why Jack is not a fan.
My previous understanding of fields comes from an expert in Australia, who has always said that static magnetic forces are not goof long term.
He mentions that pulsed is much better when the frequencies are right...
i understand the magnetico's job when working to improve ones health is to help organisation etc

discussion welcome...?

native EMF is pulsed...can we not supplement this way?

### RickD — 2014-05-08T04:36:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthpulse.10776/post-125765>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=125587)
>
> Curious to hear why Jack is not a fan.
> My previous understanding of fields comes from an expert in Australia, who has always said that static magnetic forces are not goof long term.
> He mentions that pulsed is much better when the frequencies are right...
> i understand the magnetico's job when working to improve ones health is to help organisation etc
>
> discussion welcome...?
>
> native EMF is pulsed...can we not supplement this way?

I have an Earthpulse and nothing good or bad to report from it. However, there are few things I discovered afterwards that folks should know before buying one. The AC Magnetic field EMF given off by the electro-magnet is way over 100mg when the meter is close, but falls off quickly to background in a few feet. So I wouldn't have a problem putting in under a thick mattress or on the floor beneath the bed frame, but I certainly wouldn't place it under my pillow or against my skin. The other thing to know is that Robert Becker wasn't a big fan of PEMF technology thinking it energized the cells too much and could cause cancer. In his book Crosscurrents he references a couple of early 1980s Japanese studies that showed an increase cancer cell growth for both mouse and cell cultures when exposed to PEMF.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-08T05:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthpulse.10776/post-125767>

^^^^^^This. Becker had huge issues with Bassett work on PEMF because of his unpublished work on morphogenic fields. Wertheimer work showed cancer in children 20-22% with Pulsed fields. Also, PEMF has more scalar waves........not good.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-05-08T05:34:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthpulse.10776/post-125768>

thanks guys
i do remember reading that in cross currents
i have used it on the floor under the bed, it does induce sleep very well, but this is all worth considering

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-05-08T05:35:44+0300

### EarthPulse — 2016-08-03T23:32:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthpulse.10776/post-199270>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=125770)
>
> they use a tetimonial from becker himself on the earthpulse page, but it may have been only a snippet from a larger contextual piece on the machine

Even this is an old post, but since i've just been granted access here and found this, i'll give you a frame of reference....

The larger contextual piece was that Becker said to me and my co-pilot on that 2nd trip to visit him outside Syracuse NY was... "I've never endorsed a product in my life, but i'll give you this... '''You certainly have came up with something that helps me sleep'". We snapped the picture he smoked his pipe and drank a beer, i smoked my Nat Sherman and drank mine. Poor guy just wanted his recognition for regrowing that finger tip. That came well after his books were published. Should have been on the cover of Time Magazine but it never saw the light of day.

Our Becker page -> [https://earthpulse.net/robert-o-becker/](https://earthpulse.net/robert-o-becker/) show's all the patent images that for some years were unable to be seen on the patent database due to them rigging the size of the images to some 48 inches wide version so you could only see parts of the huge images.

Our meetings were in 2004 - 2006 and i'd not figured out yet that pulsing a magnetic field (or e-stim) at these frequencies resulted in improved mitochondrial performance...so never had the benefit of presenting the NASA/Goodwin, Tong or Hood 10 Hz studies to him. It took the James Tong 2008 study for me to tie that all together.

### Joeakim — 2016-08-04T02:09:41+0300

### EarthPulse — 2016-08-04T03:10:31+0300

# Thread: home automation system
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-automation-system.10778/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### joangtaylor@gmail.com — 2014-05-07T14:47:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-automation-system.10778/post-125620>

I posed the following question on a website called "allexperts.com." I received an answer from a physics and radiation safety professor. I guess that this "expert" is not up-to-date on EMF safety.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Dear Expert,
I'm buying a house with a fancy home automation system. I'm concerned about the EMF it would generate. I think I am somewhat electro sensitive. I have tinnitus, which might be exacerbated by EMFs.
If I just turn the system off, would that stop the EMF's? Or do I need to rip it out?
Thanks.
Joan

Answer:
Tinnitus sufferers are sensitive to transformers and (more so) chopping power supplies. Really very little to do with EMFs, and a home automation system only emits absolutely tiny levels of EMF due to the ultra-low frequencies it works on. We're not talking anywhere near the same league as a single cell phone in the next house over, for most of the schemes I've seen. Don't bother turning it off, it'll manage power and actually lower the EMF levels in your house, properly used. Worry about the things that use and (especially) modify the power in your house. Flourescent lighting (the old kind with the big gas ballast, not the new little ones). Again, digital power supplies and motors. You clearly have a computer...how big is the case? I'd go to a laptop if you're concerned about that.

Lots of people think they're sensitive to such things, but currently there's absolutely no reputable (and I stress reputable, there are a lot of fear-mongers out there who have nothing better to do than espouse unproven claims) research to support this. There's a huge amount of anecdotal advice (not trustable, but very human to believe it) and some reason to believe that truly large EMFs from large transformers might have some remote biological effects.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Sorry to ask such an elementary question, but I guess that whether or not I need to tear out the home automation system depends on whether or not it emits a signal when it is unplugged. Correct?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-07T15:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-automation-system.10778/post-125622>

It emits a big enough signal to be a problem.........this guy is clueless

### nonchalant — 2014-05-07T16:44:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-automation-system.10778/post-125638>

The system will have to be ripped out, hence his defensiveness.

# Thread: Creatine and Water

**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-05-07T21:26:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creatine-and-water.10780/post-125678>

Quote from CPC 9....Comments

> D\_Ray_· [5 days ago](http://jackkruse.com/hashimotos-and-melasma/#IDComment824932277)_
>
> Relative to intra-cellular water, I've been experimenting with creatine lately to support my SAMe levels and to help with chronic sarcopenia. The positive result was a rapid weight gain of 25 pounds and better strength. However, I've read that creatine increases the overall water content in the body and changes the ratio of water between extra-cellular water and intra-cellular water in a negative way if used chronically and can lead to severe muscle cramps with intensive exercise.
>
> My experience seems to confirm that. After a while I began to get intense muscle cramps while bike riding and even while sleeping when I would stretch or change sleeping positions.
>
> Any thoughts on creatine relative to cellular hydration, i.e., good or bad?
> [jackkruse](http://jackkruse.com/)_· [5 days ago](http://jackkruse.com/hashimotos-and-melasma/#IDComment824935435)_
>
> D-Ray **_Creatine does help your cells retain water, which many meat heads think is good for performance. One huge problem.......it is not bound intracellular water to K+ as it should be in a cell. Therefore is is not used in energy transduction. It just increases mass of the muscle. What does increasing mass do? It makes you look better but it also drop energy production because of Einstein's mass equivalence equation._** So I am not fan of its use. In fact, many body builders do thing to look good and slowly kill themselves. This is one of them. Water has to be bound to proteins to get the energy benefit. One thing it is good for though it helps athletes with chronic muscle spasms.........but the effect is short lived.

### bionaut — 2014-05-24T01:12:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creatine-and-water.10780/post-128143>

I wonder if you could use it to increase intracellular bulk water and couple it with compression gear for tensegrity plus high potassium foods like spinach. You woukd incur an energy penalty and be carrying bulk water around but perhaps it could replenish lost water if you were exposed to something that would cause a drop in your proteins hydration shells. Sort of like a biological camelback for a bad environment... Need a jk to chime in here.

### Josh — 2014-05-24T01:29:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creatine-and-water.10780/post-128144>

> [persistence said:](/goto/post?id=128128)
>
> I guess everyone just talks their position on this controversial issue:
>
> [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18184753](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18184753)

There is a difference between "position" and sound quantum physiological mechanisms. I found creatine to be almost a miracle for me and my raw vegan friends and attributed it to lack of it in our diets. However, everyone plateaued and often began to decline, why? Because I am guessing we had all crashed our redox and thereby had developed methylation issues. The creatine bypassed all of this and fueled our brains and muscles for a while until we used up our methyl groups and got in new trouble...not really a "position" issue, rather a lack of sound physiological mechanistic understanding applied to various N=1's.

The abstract suggests that the authors do not understand these mechanisms well and saw enough short-term results affecting the things they listed, that they concluded that the cramping isuue, etc. was not a problem. Well I did cramp a great deal and needed potassium and magnesium to balance it out. Again, why? My mitochondria were shot and I did not have enough magnesium and or potassium in my system. I find studies such as this are too broad to be of any use for people with challenged N=1, and dangerous for the longterm viability of healthier people because they support things without appearing to drill down to the fundamental physiology.

Far and away the most dramatic imporvements I have seen with creatine have been cognitive and as Dr. Kruse points out, it is an expensive fuel to use.

### bionaut — 2014-05-24T01:51:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creatine-and-water.10780/post-128145>

Part of the reason that supplements can sometimes be problematic. We do not know what we do not know :/. Thanks for the cautionary tale.

### persistence — 2014-05-24T01:58:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creatine-and-water.10780/post-128147>

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=128144)
>
> There is a difference between "position" and sound quantum physiological mechanisms. I found creatine to be almost a miracle for me and my raw vegan friends and attributed it to lack of it in our diets. However, everyone plateaued and often began to decline, why? Because I am guessing we had all crashed our redox and thereby had developed methylation issues. The creatine bypassed all of this and fueled our brains and muscles for a while until we used up our methyl groups and got in new trouble...not really a "position" issue, rather a lack of sound physiological mechanistic understanding applied to various N=1's.
>
> The abstract suggests that the authors do not understand these mechanisms well and saw enough short-term results affecting the things they listed, that they concluded that the cramping isuue, etc. was not a problem. Well I did cramp a great deal and needed potassium and magnesium to balance it out. Again, why? My mitochondria were shot and I did not have enough magnesium and or potassium in my system. I find studies such as this are too broad to be of any use for people with challenged N=1, and dangerous for the longterm viability of healthier people because they support things without appearing to drill down to the fundamental physiology.
>
> Far and away the most dramatic imporvements I have seen with creatine have been cognitive and as Dr. Kruse points out, it is an expensive fuel to use.

I agree with the spirit of this, but maybe you are making this more difficult than it needs to be. Creatine may just be causing you to hold more water and diluting electrolytes in the process. So take more of the extracellular sodium and more of the intracellular potassium to keep things in balance, and the body just shed the excess beyond its real needs. Probably getting some magnesium and some bicarbonates into the electrolyte solution would be important if you wanted a really balanced electrolyte.

Like you, I sense that the cramping is due to an electrolyte balance issue. Until my own health crisis of the last six months, I had no idea that electrolytes were so critical to health and even a small imbalance can leave you virtually disabled.

You can create thousands of intelligent speculations based on understanding chemical processes. That's not a double blind study. Most theories don't test as theorized.

Rather than reading about methylation pathways and speculating on leading edge biochemistry, how about running a good electrolyte panel that shows your current values for:

sodium
potassium
chloride
CO2 = bicarbonates
magnesium
calcium

And if you want to get fancy and spend a bit more money, add Magnesium RBC and Potassium RBC to get the intracellular values of those two.

My guess is that 90% of the time muscle cramps will show up in some way in that test and probably correcting the electrolyte imbalance directly will fix it.

Resort to fancy science only after simple science fails to solve the problem.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-village-where-people-melt-away.10782/post-125708>

**The village where people melt away: Brazilian farming community made up of skin disease sufferers for whom working in the sun is a way of life... and potentially fatal**

-   **Araras has largest group of people suffering from xeroderma pigmentosum**
-   **Those with disease, also known as XP, are sensitive to ultraviolet rays**
-   **Is a burden for town in country's midwest where outdoor work is vital**
-   **Twenty people in the population of 800 are said to have genetic condition**

Read more: [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2621553/The-village-people-melt-away-Brazilian-farming-community-skin-disease-sufferers-working-sun-way-life-potentially-fatal.html#ixzz3144kQVfY](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2621553/The-village-people-melt-away-Brazilian-farming-community-skin-disease-sufferers-working-sun-way-life-potentially-fatal.html#ixzz3144kQVfY)

### john0713 — 2014-05-08T12:18:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-particle-issues.10783/post-125805>

I need help understanding why my LDL P and small particle counts are so extremely high and testosterone low. I usually eat between 40 and 70g of carbs per day and keep my daily calories to between 1700 and 2200 (6' 2" male, 206 lbs). I do crossfit 2x per week, sprint once a week, walk 5 to 6 miles a day. I don't snack, don't drink caffeine, and drink one or two glasses of wine a week. All beef is grass fed, eat seafood 4 or 5 times per week, and don't have any issues with excess appetite, so I normally eat two meals per day. I've been on my diet for about 5 months and have lost 4" from my waist and about 40 pounds. Generally I feel extremely healthy. I supplement with lugols iodine, 10 drops per day, transdermal magnesium, 40 sprays per day, 5,000 IU of D3 on days I don't get enough sun, a one a day vitamin, and fish oil.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-05-08T13:22:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-particle-issues.10783/post-125819>

ditch crossfit and any sort of stressor.
if the ASI shows altered cortisol, it is most likely pregnenolone steal syndrome. its in one of jacks blogs, [http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/](http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/)

here is a main quote

Leptin resistance occurs first. Then insulin resistance happens next. This eventually leads to adrenal resistance. Cortisol is the stress hormone that allows for fight or flight syndrome (life or death). This is the hormone that allows you to run away from a hungry lion fast and live. Evolution always makes sure cortisol production stays ready for action at the expense of the other hormones that also are made from the same precursors. That precursor is pregnenolone. Pregnenolone is made from cholesterol, and cholesterol is made from LDL. So anytime the body is stressed or inflamed, it up-regulates cholesterol production to make more lifesaving hormones. It requires T3 and vitamin A as cofactors to complete this step. Blocking cholesterol production will increase cellular stress. This is why biochemically, to me, no statin drugs have ever made any sense under any circumstance in medicine. Moreover, this is why there is a chronic association of cancer to low cholesterol levels in the literature. If your cholesterol levels are low, you can not proper construct a mitotic spindle to pull apart your chromosomes correctly. This is why cancer rates have been shown to be higher in 11 studies on statins. This has been documented in the literature and the Great Cholesterol Con, by Anthony Colpo. In times of infection or stress, LDL levels always need to rise to protect the cell to make more cholesterol to make hormones and improve intracellular signaling.

i would start by ditching physical stressors like HIT/crossfit

### Melanie Procter — 2014-05-12T12:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-particle-issues.10783/post-126426>

I was reading the Tilted Quilt post as I was researching cholesterol. It seems as it is important to both generate and hold a charge I think it would be interesting to do daily long term readings on ph to measure your "proton presence". In the Tilted Quilt blog, Jack wrote, "pH is a measure of proton presence. Protons are acids. Positively charged (+) protons gobble up the negative (-) electrons in our body to turn oxygen into water in our mitochondria. Failed oxygen delivery to tissues is the beginning of a spiral of descent into neolithic disease. As the photoelectric charge becomes reduced, less and less oxygen penetrates cells, creating bad news for you, and good news for your doctor!
Stress lowers your pH because you are losing electrons to your environment in the form of bio-photons."

This is starting to make sense to us now due to current blogs.

### john0713 — 2014-06-11T01:49:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-particle-issues.10783/post-130432>

Sorry, for the ASI (free cortisol rhythm) it shows just nM. For DHEA it shows ng/ml, for progesterone pg/ml, for Total Salivary SIgA mg/dL

For the recommended values on this scale for cortisol my report lists:

morning: 13 to 24
noon: 5 to 10
evening: 3 to 8
night: 1 to 4

So it seems scaled similarly. I'm just not sure what nM means as opposed to ng/mL. On this document: [http://www.becomehealthynow.com/pdf...techs/lab\_test\_examples/asi\_sample\_report.pdf](http://www.becomehealthynow.com/pdf/products_services/diagnostechs/lab_test_examples/asi_sample_report.pdf) if you look at page 12 my report looks like that but with the values I posted.

# Thread: On the thermodynamics of comfort
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-thermodynamics-of-comfort.10786/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 159
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NeilBB — 2014-05-08T16:18:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-thermodynamics-of-comfort.10786/post-125856>

Jack is always talking about "getting comfortable with your discomfort." I've been thinking about this from a thermodynamic perspective...

For a living organism, the idea of “chronically seeking comfort” is extremely problematic because comfort is nothing but a state of psychological ease or equilibrium, which for a human means physical and mental relaxation, which if practiced chronically means the loss of the ability to adapt, stagnation, and then illness, atrophy, and eventually permanent equilibrium, better known as death.

By contrast, embracing discomfort as a policy means remaining in an "athletic mental posture," ready to respond to any current or future challenge, known or unknown. It means placing the laws of the universe and the requirements of life ahead of one’s habits, wants, and desires. It is the policy of psychological adaptability and metastability. The psychological analog to the living state. One's acceptance of the responsibility implied by the laws of thermodynamics. Humans must aggressively avoid equilibrium in every way and seek metastability instead.

Gretchen, why is the most depraved individual the one without a purpose? Because he values comfort more than productiveness. He increases rather than decreases both his own entropy and also the entropy of humanity. Productiveness really boils down to adaptability and metastability. Life requires movement and action. Physically and mentally. Literally and figuratively. Comfort is like a vacation. OK in small doses, but not as a lifetime aspiration.

In medicine, "comfort" is really the tool of hospice, applicable to the dying, not the living. Yet our current medical system emphasizes satisfaction surveys over the hard reality of valid therapy, and pills over lifestyle adaptation. The entire modern world, in fact, happens to be built around the idea of maximizing the "comfort and convenience of the masses," regardless of consequences. It is basically the organizing principle of modern economic consumerism... Think about it... And then just look around to see the consequences…

The "dream" that we have all been sold by the modern marketing machine is actually a nightmare which has blunted us to what it really means to have the responsibility of living. That responsibility is not about comfort, it is about metastability.

### Lahelada — 2014-05-08T16:53:09+0300

### SeaHorse — 2014-05-08T17:02:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-thermodynamics-of-comfort.10786/post-125873>

I agree with this and feel there is resource that waits for those beyond the "posturing"…..a sense of connection and exhilaration and harmony that come about with all the moving and changing that you describe. So in the end the discomfort leads to something far greater than an appeasement of pain by modern means.

I'm not sure that "productiveness" is a helpful term…I know what you mean, but it is a kind of manufacturing metaphor…I like how you link responsibility or responsiveness with metastability…..less protestant work ethic, more laws of nature. We are holding up our end of the universe so to speak.

I wonder If we are really entering a different universe when we sleep by tuning in a different frequency? 'weak' to say, but I remember that when I was a child all these things in nature fascinated me, the whole experience of being in the beach and seeing the living things that it has evoked me strong feelings, like flavours... that hit me very strongly, I would come back home and devour books about marine life, paint them... in my latest years I feel like I forgot about all these things, as if I've been unpluged to tune in the automated consciousness that society has created for us... only recently I seem to start thinking again for myself and tune more with nature... yet not so strongly as when I was a child. 'weak' to say, but I remember that when I was a child all these things in nature fascinated me, the whole experience of being in the beach and seeing the living things that it has evoked me strong feelings, like flavours... that hit me very strongly, I would come back home and devour books about marine life, paint them... in my latest years I feel like I forgot about all these things, as if I've been unpluged to tune in the automated consciousness that society has created for us... only recently I seem to start thinking again for myself and tune more with nature... yet not so strongly as when I was a child. I get so excited about the nature all the time Albert.. i cannot get enough of it really! I can just be on the pasture for hours and do nothing... just listen to the birds and watch the lambs... watch the heaven and my surroundings - i could do it forever
I just was out to let the sheep eat before the night on the neighbors pasture and I ate plenty of wild herbs too, just the ram who followed me closely wanted to eat the out of my mouth LOL

So, I wonder about something too that have happened to me quite some times.
If I have had a rough day with big mental challenges, and I have faced them and the pain, i then in the following night have had the most awesome dreams, very sensual, seriously strong oxy dreams and I wake up feeling amazing!
It have happened so many times I nowadays tell myself if times get rough - ahhh, do not worry, just do the right things (and the right thing tend to be the hardest, darn!) and you will be rewarded in your dreams...

I really wonder why this is?

### SeaHorse — 2014-05-08T22:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-thermodynamics-of-comfort.10786/post-125916>

I've been thinking about these ideas around discomfort and sleep and then started wondering about trauma. Animals who have access to their natural environments and can express their species behaviours often have lives full of trauma…prey animals particularly. But their nervous systems allow for the "excess energy" to disperse after the flight, fight, freeze period is over. They know what to do and they do not stay traumatized…it would lead to extinction. Domestic animals are another story.

We western humans are educated out of knowing how to dispel and overcome trauma naturally. Our traumas are maintained in our nervous systems through our belief systems about appropriate behaviours and sanctioned therapies. I think this is getting exponentially worse as we lose health individually and as a species.
This connects in with the difficulties of experiencing discomfort and some of the victim mentality that exists in the health field. (Just to be clear, I'm not discounting the devastation that trauma carries or talking about cultures that have more successful ways of dealing with trauma i.e. being in nature)

So how does this tie into sleep??
It's often only during sleep, at the unconscious level or the cellular level that we can find or access the traumas we carry around.….if we start feeding ourselves more electrons and building a metastable system of integrated health….isn't our nervous system going to naturally start wanting to kick these blockages out the door? This might involve a lot of discomfort, but isn't that how the laws of nature work in all animals? So once again our brains distinguish us from the rest of the animal kingdom. We build such elaborate intellectual constructs and cultures they end up taking us away from the innate wisdom we are born with and operate under according to nature.

So Inger…perhaps you are dreaming the way all humans should…for recovery and dispersal of "negative" energy experienced during the day rather than the kind of nightmares that so many people have where they are dipping into their unprocessed pain which they constantly carry within.

Phew…I have no idea if that makes any sense put pressing the send button anyway...

### NeilBB — 2014-05-09T01:49:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-thermodynamics-of-comfort.10786/post-125953>

> In medicine, "comfort" is really the tool of hospice, applicable to the dying, not the living. Yet our current medical system emphasizes satisfaction surveys over the hard reality of valid therapy, and pills over lifestyle adaptation

I wasn't thinking this when I wrote the above, but actually the current Western medical approach to all chronic disease is **already a prolonged "hospice care" mentality:** _Use drugs to keep patients as "comfortable and symptom-free" as possible until they die, since we really haven't the first clue how to make them healthy._ **This approach acknowledges from the start that we are all basically sick and dying and that's just the way it is. That is the given that is built in to the model of modern medicine.**

Pretty much universally, insurers, doctors and patients have all accepted this approach and no alternative is even thought to be possible. To even consider that an actual _restoration of health_ is an attainable goal is a very quick ticket to being branded a quack or charlatan. That simply does not fit the current medical paradigm. It's no wonder that despite all its flaws and inconsistencies, alternative medicine has exploded recently. At least it considers good health to be a _goal_. The medical system now seeks to employ not healers, but glorified "hospice doctors" whether we like it or not. That's what the system demands and what most of the patients actually prefer. And **that** may be the hardest pill of all to swallow.

And actually, I don't mind being a "hospice doctor" because I really do like helping people, even if it is just relieving their pain and symptoms. There is a lot of good to still do even in that limited area, but it's just so sad: (1) that we can't really fix people and (2) that most don't even really seem to want to be fixed or think that it's even possible to be fixed. It almost breaks my heart that most patient's expectations for improvement have become so modest these days. No one really _expects to get better_ anymore. They are resigned to their fate. So its not surprising that "seeking comfort "becomes a substitute for seeking the "impossible" cure.

But perhaps without the knowledge of the quantum mechanism, this is the best that Western medicine could have ever been expected to do. Very possible. Its knowledge base has always been compartmentalized, fragmented and disconnected, and without any unifying theory of health, just based on observation of disease and trial and error mostly. So maybe that is why it has had to set such modest, disjointed, and limited goals and why it is at a dead end now. And without such a unifying theory, medical science has really never had any hope to understand the mechanistic effect of the "process of modernization" in creating our "diseases of civilization." So, it hasn't even bothered to really try. It just accepts the _existence of the diseases of civilization_ (not to mention the ecological collapse of the planet) as the given, the "initial condition" of today's problem, not to be questioned and far beyond our power to alter. And it follows logically from that and from basic epidemiology that the entire modern world would now be thought of as a gigantic hospice clinic. So organized medicine offers comfort-care and symptom-relief because it's the best we think we can do.

All of that would seem to indicate that it is about time for the quantum mechanism to establish a new foundation for medical _healing_, so that it might actually one day become something more than just the relief of modern suffering!!! I hope it's not already too late.
