### nonchalant — 2014-05-26T19:31:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-doesnt-work.10924/post-128418>

I seem to recall Jack saying that once a threshold is reached, the large charges will move. Either from the earth to the body, or, perhaps more often, from the body to the earth. Probably the tickling feeling we often get.

For the small negative charge from the earth, your tissues have to be ready to move the charge. Semiconductors charged and ready. Redox potential up.

### sjoshua — 2014-06-25T03:37:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-doesnt-work.10924/post-132110>

Thought experiment:

If you try to plug an American lamp into a UK outlet, you will run into problems. First off, the plug is a different shape, and will not interface. Second, the frequency of AC currents varies throughout the world, so the 60Hz American lamp may not appreciate the 50Hz UK frequency. Third, the voltage output varies as well, so the 120v American lamp would not operate on the 230v UK circuits.

Hopefully this mismatch provides you with a rough example of why humans have difficulty with the concept of grounding. When you do not match the environment you are in, then you cannot expect the power source to interface properly. So when humans don't match the environment of the earth, then even if the ability to ground is there, its like trying to plug the American lamp into the UK socket... Interface properly, and you can recharge at will.

# Thread: I have a hell of a story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-hell-of-a-story.10925/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 128
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2014-05-26T11:06:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-hell-of-a-story.10925/post-128393>

But I'll try to keep it as brief as possible.

I'm 29, was always active, was working as a personal trainer and competitive athlete. At 26 I got hit with adrenal fatigue and it got bad to the point where I was near Addisons level. I was put on 20 mg hydrocortisone and started to slowly recover until I was given ativan for my sleep problems. In just 2 weeks I became nearly catatonic from the ativan so they pulled me off it- bad idea. I spun into the most horrible withdrawals imaginable- I had over 70 symptoms including; depersonalization, 20 hour long panic attacks, seizures, sleeping 5 hours/week, relentless anxiety, tinnitus, depression etc... It is now 2 years from that point and I still haven't recovered. Maybe 50%

Anyway, after 1 year of this I was told to try phosphatidylserine for my horrible sleep problems. I took only 1 pill and woke up that night with (what was later diagnosed as) encephalitis- massive dissociation, memory loss, cognitive loss, seizures (again), intense personality changes, psychosis, and my heart rate slowed down into the 30's. My dumb ass had been through so much at that point I didn't even go to the ER (I was admitted to the hospital several months later and a lumbar puncture found white blood cells)

So to add to it the encephalitis triggered M.E.

I've since been going downhill and nothing has stopped it. If I exert even the slightest bit of energy it will permanently progress my disease. I now deal with; massive dissociation, psychosis, dementia, memory loss, cognitive loss, anxiety, fatigue, pain, horrid headaches, insomnia, dysautonomia, light sensitivity and an overwhelming feeling of toxicity..... just to name a few.

I have had tons of tests done and the biggest ones of note are: low NK cell function, low ADH, high LP-PLA2, high levels of lead, EEG slowing, high titres for m. pneumoniae and parvo, low ESR, high ferritin and widespread hypoperfusion to my brain on a SPECT scan. I had an MRI and MRA of my brain also. Both of which both came back clear.

An odd thing of note also- since the M.E. has hit my adrenal fatigue is as good as gone. Poof- went away just like that. I've even done a couple saliva cortisol tests and compared to my earlier ones my cortisol has gone UP!

They thought I may have an autoimmune encephalitis so my doctor checked my neopterin and ANA and they came back normal- he said that means I don't have brain inflammation.

Other random tests...
8-OXO-deoxyguanosine: normal
8-OXO- guanosine: normal

I very recently had one band come back positive for Lyme so I will be digging into that further as well. I have a bunch of SNP's (including MTHFR +/+) and methylation defects as well.

I'm a mess. If I read my story I probably wouldn't even believe it's all true, but it is, 100% of it. I am shocked that the lifestyle I led allowed me to get so sick. I even ate Paleo for about 10 years before ever getting ill. I've seen so many doctors and they get so flustered, they have no idea what to do with me. Physically I'm in bad shape, yes, but my brain- my brain is just so far gone- I have no other way to describe it.

I'm currently seeing a doctor who did a cellular membrane lipid test on me and it came back pretty bad so she wants me to start on phosphatidylcholine. That's the only lead I have at the moment. I'm really looking for some outside the box thinking which is why I'm here. I'll do absolutely anything it takes- anything. I loved my life and I'll fight to get it back.

Thanks to anyone who read this.

### caroline — 2014-05-26T11:30:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-hell-of-a-story.10925/post-128394>

Hi Aerose ...and welcome.
My heart goes out to you......but at least you have come to the right place!

can you post your labs? there is a lab section and someone will be along to help with thoughts.

Have you managed to read any of the blogs?
Have you listened to the podcasts? they are a quick and easier way to get information - they are on the home page.

do you have Dr. K.'s book? ...The Epi-Paleo RX - it is a faster way to understand a lot of this.

What is your non native EMF exposure? your house? your neighbours house? smart meters? power lines? cell towers?

Are you able to get oustside in the sunshine? put barefeet on the ground? wear as little clothes as possible!

You need to be drinking good bottled water.

Limit computer time ...it is stealing electrons from you.

What about where you sleep? there should be no electronics at all ..... no TV, no clock radio, no ipad, no iphone

Can you cut the breaker to your bedroom?

where do you live?

details matter ....

what do you eat?

Please don't hesitate to ask questions ....there are no stupid questions!

If you need help with anything - just holler!

I am sending you a huge hug ......Your life needs to get better ....... you are young and we will al help you anyway we can.

I am sure that Dr. Kruse would like to see your labs ......and I am also sure that he will pop in!

maybe read the blog about Jeremy Thomley - he is an incredible inspiration to all of us

### Nick — 2014-05-26T11:41:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-hell-of-a-story.10925/post-128396>

Hi Aerose,

That is a hell of a story and it sounds like you have been to hell and back. I think you will find a lot of answers in this forum, Caroline has given you heaps of major clues about where to start. The good news is the prescriptions should be easy to do to get yourself sorted. The bad news is that your battery is flat and it will take a couple of years to bounce back from here, but bounce back you should be able to do! You will need to be all over the details as you learn them.

I suspect you have been smashed by non native EMF along the way, it is such an insidious thing! A critical start point is to remove as much as that as you can immediately, especially where you sleep. Then quickly grab blue blocker glasses for night and start going to bed early, then get lots and lots of Reverse Osmosis water into you.

After that there are heap of areas such as sunlight, grounding, CT, eating times etc etc that you will learn heaps about here. I'm not a Dr but I am sure the ones that are will jump in with more or better advice.

Get your hands on as many as JK's interviews and podcasts as you can. They are a great source of information.

Good Luck!

### Aerose91 — 2014-05-26T11:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-hell-of-a-story.10925/post-128397>

Thank you guys so much for the kind replies.

I guess I should mention things I've done so far

I ground every day and sleep on an earthing mat

I have blue light filters on all my lights and on my computer if I need to use it.

My circadian rhythms have been pulverized by this disease. I can't fall asleep till about 8 am and sleep Maybe 3-4 broken hours. I've tried everything under the sun to change this but nothing helps.

I've read all the blogs here and have been trying that eating schedule. Unfortunately my digestion is so poor I get extremely sick if I eat too much in one meal and my hypoglycemia is so bad if I don't eat right before bed I will be shot awake with adrenaline rushes all night. Everything in my body is so disregulated and keeping my rhythms stable doesn't help it- it's like there's something disrupting my brains rhythms.

I'd be happy to post my labs and would love if Dr Kruse could look at my story. I have trouble retaining.info with my brain the way it is so i just can't figure out where to start and put together a protocol

### Aerose91 — 2014-05-26T20:12:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-hell-of-a-story.10925/post-128423>

Well I'm from connecticut but the past decade was living in L.A. Since I've been so sick however, I've moved back to CT. I'm in the country.

My circadian rhythms were never great- I've always been a night person, but literally the instant this disease hit everything in my brain went out of whack. I would fall asleep around 12 and wake up at 4 am. Then I would sleep at 2 am and wake up at 5 am. Now it's fall asleep around 8 am and sleep poorly till about 1 or 2pm. Honestly all the best sleep hygiene in the world isn't strong enough to counter whatever is happening to my brain- it just hasnt worked. I also have very bad orthostatic intolerance to the point where if I have to walk more than a few blocks I require a wheelchair.

I have an earthing mat that you plug in to the ground wire of an outlet but during the day I spend as much time outside without shoes as possible. Its all green where I am. The mat is just for sleeping. We also only have 1 computer in our house but it is turned off at night. The closest cell tower I can think if is about 30 miles away and I'm not aware if any nearby radio towers

I drink only spring water that I get myself from the spring (which has been tested) and I drink no less than a gallon a day with sea salt added. I use only natural soaps and toothpaste without fluoride.

Nights are tough because I lay awake all night (man am I sick of watching the sun rise) and since I'm not even close to sleeping I have to find things to do. First I meditate for a while hoping it will put me out, then I lay there staring at the wall for a while, then I'll get up and eat (cuz I have to) and read a book or sonething. Then usually it's just tossing and turning for hours on end. Usually as well when I do sleep Its just packed full of odd vivid dreams and nightmares so that makes me exhausted when I get up in the morning. The nightmares started after a period of over exertion several months ago.

I would love to post my labs but I have a TON. Should I post them all anyway? I'm happy to do so.

Thanks again everyone, you've already been so welcoming.

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/). I would love to sign up for a higher level membership but I literally don't have the money. I havent worked in several years and the debt collectors are drowning me with all my medical bills. I can't even afford to keep seeing my doctors at this point.

@[Nick](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/3014/). My bun/creatinine ratio has always come back normal. I can tell you I'm dehydrated though because of the Low ADH. No matter how much water I drink I don't seem to retain it. Also never been tested for EMF- how do you do that?

I am tirelessly reading through everything on this site and the forums because I'm trying to absorb all of this great info. Unfortunately though, my brain doesn't allow me to retain much these days. On bad days I can't even remember how to tie my shoes =(
Believe it or not I used to be smart, though! I was first in my class in microbiology and in the top 1% ranked personal trainers in the country. I just can't nearly match those things any more. I'm here hoping that I can get some help organising all of this info and putting together a structured protocol because I just can't figure out where to start.

Also I should apologize for how scatter-brained my posts probably are- my writing skills have declined considerably and by the end of a paragraph ive already forgotten what I wrote in the beginning of it, so I apologize if my posts aren't as legible as they should be.

I have to eat every couple of hours so if I'm awake at night I have to eat as well. My hypoglycemia is so bad that if I don't I won't sleep 1 minute but rather be kept up all night by intense adrenaline rushes and panic attacks.

I would agree with Josh on this. In your situation you are going to need help to see this through, the protocols on this site can help your redox but you will also need recurring lab analysis and a doc in your court. You have symptoms of transition metal concentration as evidenced by the lead and iron in your body, this would cause sensitivity to emf and also sunlight. MTHFR defects would exacerbate this and make metal removal difficult. Your redox potential is definitely altered I wouldn't doubt if you have huge IL6 issues tanking your DHEA, your sleep shows this and redox issues are hinted at by the rough interactions you have to many drugs.

Be aware the CT easy start protocol has a disclaimer on it that you should heed and proceed very slowly, you are definitely a special case.

If you are not abject to the following foods I would try eating parsley, cilantro and brazil nuts to assist with metal chelation. Turmeric, garlic, paprika, to assist with inflammation and mushrooms for folate. Oysters are the best if you can handle em.

Compression clothing will assist you with the retention of water. Adding it on acts to put pressure on the bodies tensegrity grid. This favors water retention in the bodies cytoarchitecture.

If you are burning in the sun I would limit to the milder times until you continue to improve.

There is a CSF flow that affects sleep. By cooling the carotid arteries you can assist the body to release melatonin from the pineal gland. You can start by putting a cool washcloth on your neck before bed. There are also sleep targeted meditation techniques you can google to help calm your body as you try and get rest as well as oxytocin release, I am sure you can figure out what that is.

If you are emf sensitive then connecting to the mat to the ground in your house is likely pwning you. the body is sensitive and responds well from the naturally occuring 7hz-40hz resonance. Our outlets start at 60hz and spike depending on the time of day. If you can sleep in a dark basement that doesn't set off your mold then go for it.

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=128451)
>
> Large amounts of energy cannot be controlled.

I wonder if your brain has demyelinated a signifigant amount? However, I would have expected that shown up on a brain scan...but alas I am not a brain surgeon yet. So I do not know. There are diets where children exhibiting some of the symptoms you do eat a strict water and fat diet to remyelinate such as children do, however with your level of hypoglycemia I do not know how you would be able to even fathom this. Just out of curiosity are you able to tolerate coconut well? The MCT in it should be beneficial in making ketones to bring down brain inflammation. I hope this helps.

> In terms of oxytocin- I believe I read in one of Dr Kruse's blogs that oxytocin release in the morning is best, especially for those with adrenal fatigue? (which I have) Correct me if I'm wrong there.
>
> I've read through pretty much all of his blogs and it's such a wealth of information- I just have so much trouble organising and retaining new info so I'm a bit flustered on where to start. I'm reading about the adrenal fatigue rx, redox potential and c/t but I can't quite put all of it together in a protocol for myself. If I have 5 papers in front of me with dates in them I can't even organise the dates chronologically- sux.
> So I appreciate all the help you guys are giving me. I'm really, really wiped out from a few minutes of sun yesterday so I can't do anything for a while but I look forward to starting up something new and see if how it works

Look, there is a lot here... all kinds of things, people are laying on magnets and shooting lasers up their noses. There are many N=1. You need to just start wherever "you" are. Don't worry about implementing it all right now. It is like layers of an onion. The best thing you can do is start a journal and write. As you reflect on incremental changes you will be able to find out what works for you. You have a unique issue... we all do. This will help you stay focused and see where you have been. There are many starfish here to help you on your way.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-28T05:24:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-hell-of-a-story.10925/post-128651>

> [Aerose91 said:](/goto/post?id=128393)
>
> But I'll try to keep it as brief as possible.
>
> I'm 29, was always active, was working as a personal trainer and competitive athlete. At 26 I got hit with adrenal fatigue and it got bad to the point where I was near Addisons level. I was put on 20 mg hydrocortisone and started to slowly recover until I was given ativan for my sleep problems. In just 2 weeks I became nearly catatonic from the ativan so they pulled me off it- bad idea. I spun into the most horrible withdrawals imaginable- I had over 70 symptoms including; depersonalization, 20 hour long panic attacks, seizures, sleeping 5 hours/week, relentless anxiety, tinnitus, depression etc... It is now 2 years from that point and I still haven't recovered. Maybe 50%
>
> Anyway, after 1 year of this I was told to try phosphatidylserine for my horrible sleep problems. I took only 1 pill and woke up that night with (what was later diagnosed as) encephalitis- massive dissociation, memory loss, cognitive loss, seizures (again), intense personality changes, psychosis, and my heart rate slowed down into the 30's. My dumb ass had been through so much at that point I didn't even go to the ER (I was admitted to the hospital several months later and a lumbar puncture found white blood cells)
>
> So to add to it the encephalitis triggered M.E.
>
> I've since been going downhill and nothing has stopped it. If I exert even the slightest bit of energy it will permanently progress my disease. I now deal with; massive dissociation, psychosis, dementia, memory loss, cognitive loss, anxiety, fatigue, pain, horrid headaches, insomnia, dysautonomia, light sensitivity and an overwhelming feeling of toxicity..... just to name a few.
>
> I have had tons of tests done and the biggest ones of note are: low NK cell function, low ADH, high LP-PLA2, high levels of lead, EEG slowing, high titres for m. pneumoniae and parvo, low ESR, high ferritin and widespread hypoperfusion to my brain on a SPECT scan. I had an MRI and MRA of my brain also. Both of which both came back clear.
>
> An odd thing of note also- since the M.E. has hit my adrenal fatigue is as good as gone. Poof- went away just like that. I've even done a couple saliva cortisol tests and compared to my earlier ones my cortisol has gone UP!
>
> They thought I may have an autoimmune encephalitis so my doctor checked my neopterin and ANA and they came back normal- he said that means I don't have brain inflammation.
>
> Other random tests...
> 8-OXO-deoxyguanosine: normal
> 8-OXO- guanosine: normal
>
> I very recently had one band come back positive for Lyme so I will be digging into that further as well. I have a bunch of SNP's (including MTHFR +/+) and methylation defects as well.
>
> I'm a mess. If I read my story I probably wouldn't even believe it's all true, but it is, 100% of it. I am shocked that the lifestyle I led allowed me to get so sick. I even ate Paleo for about 10 years before ever getting ill. I've seen so many doctors and they get so flustered, they have no idea what to do with me. Physically I'm in bad shape, yes, but my brain- my brain is just so far gone- I have no other way to describe it.
>
> I'm currently seeing a doctor who did a cellular membrane lipid test on me and it came back pretty bad so she wants me to start on phosphatidylcholine. That's the only lead I have at the moment. I'm really looking for some outside the box thinking which is why I'm here. I'll do absolutely anything it takes- anything. I loved my life and I'll fight to get it back.
>
> Thanks to anyone who read this.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-a-hell-of-a-story.10925/post-128884>

**Key Geek Point:** Thus, the PVN, unlike any other brain site, has direct influence over both sympathetic and parasympathetic outflow. Furthermore, the PVN receives direct sympathetic and parasympathetic afferent inputs from trigeminal pars caudalis (sympathetic) and the nucleus of the solitary tract (parasympathetic). This is why we use face dunks in the [Cold Thermogenesis Protocol](http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/). It is the fastest way to signal an inhibited a broken PVN. (a bad ASI) The PVN therefore is the only brain site in a closed efferent-afferent reflex loop with both the sympathetic and parasympathetic nervous systems. **This implies that the PVN is the key portion to study in the brain in all cases of adrenal fatigue. The system breaks when signaling is broken here. The PVN is a collector of environmental inputs. So if we can’t sense our environment well the output of the PVN will not be reflective if what our brain is really experiencing in life.**
**

### Penny — 2014-05-30T19:23:15+0300

Yup. You have Lyme. Melatonin signals Lyme to come alive - my guess as to why your sleep is in the toilet - Tim Jackson is your man. He cured himself of Lyme - no idea how - but, since you're broke, Dr. Nancy Mullan might be able to help you for free with her phone calls on Tuesday nights - [www.drnancymullan.com](http://www.drnancymullan.com) - lots of stuff to read about on her chronic disease website also -

As a microbiologist - awesome BTW - you know that Lyme is pleomorphic - i.e. it's a shape changer - it sheds its outer wall (cell wall deficient bacteria) which makes it undetectable to the immune system - it also uses manganese as a biofilm so lactoferrin won't help - although it would probably help with all the god forsaken friends the Lyme is cohabitating with...

You eat in the middle of the night because the parasite is hungry - I went to dramyyasko.com where there is a forum and typed "lyme" and got this response:

"A future consideration may be to look at addressing the possibility of chronic Lyme infection in the body. Lyme disease has been implicated in a number of neurological conditions. Cat's claw is reported to be helpful for viral issues as well as for Lyme. Artemisia (wormwood) has also shown activity against Lyme. Wormwood is a component of Paradex that was already mentioned with respect to other parasitic infections. In addition, tick support RNAs and IMF 2 should help to support the body. Chronic issues withLyme may overlap with thyroid dysfunction."

So, since the little buggers never travel alone, can chat with each other and actually know when they've reached critical mass through quorum sensing, you could try the following: Buy some Paradex and Cat's Claw and take it before bed - you should notice a change - I have done this for yeast and whatever other crappy bacteria I harbor because of years of needless antibiotics before going to the dentist and it's amazing how it helps your sleep. Dr. Nancy Mullan said whenever sleep is in the toilet, she thinks yeast...

This forum chats about your phosphatidyl serine issue - it's a methyl donor so you probably overmethylated which leads to more glutamates which leads to excitotoxicity and neuronal death -
[http://forums.phoenixrising.me/inde...ome-foursome-phosphatidyl-serine.13974/page-4](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/the-awesome-foursome-phosphatidyl-serine.13974/page-4)

And this one goes into more depth about overmethylation and glutamates:

[http://forums.phoenixrising.me/inde...ion-protocol-revised-as-of-today.9447/page-32](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/simplified-methylation-protocol-revised-as-of-today.9447/page-32)

Here is a list of excitotoxins you want to avoid or at least limit:
[http://www.dramyyasko.com/wp-content/files\_flutter/1279663001Neuroprovokers8.pdf](http://www.dramyyasko.com/wp-content/files_flutter/1279663001Neuroprovokers8.pdf)

Naturally, stress will also generate excitotoxicity, so you really want to try to limit that -

And if you're taking vitamins, if the word "methyl" appears, it's a methyl donor - Yasko says to save the methyl donors for things like coq10 and SAM-e -

So, the cheapest way to keep the calcium from flooding the neurons is to soak in Epsom Salts - the magnesium keeps the calcium at bay and the sulfates act like a toxin sponge -

You can also take B6 in the form of P5P, vitamin D, vitamin K2, and zinc - we have been using zinc with carnosine (PepsinGI) twice a day because the carnosine holds the zinc in place longer so it helps seal the gut lining - this will limit the steady stream of garbage coming from your gut into your bloodstream, thereby overwhelming your immune system - which is by now also in the toilet - B6 and K2 also help convert gluatmates to GABA - and you can also take (GABA Calm) -

The cheapest way to bring up the immune system is to take colostrum - think of it as providing alternate immunoglobulins while your own is busy... Dr. Kendahl Stewart has some great videos on youtube about how to bring up the immune system because the other part of these microbes is they disable the immune system:

As far as enzymes in general, you want to provide the minerals you are missing and get rid of the toxic metals - I just got an $85.00 hair test from holisticheal.com and that way I also got Dr. Amy's comments on the letter I wrote with it and her advice - she'll want to know your genetics of course, so you should get that from 23andme.com and then run it through knowyourgenetics.com -

So, the general immune system protocol would be vitamin C(ascorbate), vitamin E, colostrum, zinc with carnosine and a mineral formula without iron/copper - I use Thorne Citramins II - I would also add milk thistle for your liver - in fact, Liverguard isn't a bad formula for liver support with a pretty good mix of b vitamins -

Some cheap ways of getting rid of aluminum are magnesium malate (malic acid) and horsetail (silica in it binds to aluminum) and for the mercury she recommended wasabi japonica -

If you have the MTHFR 1298, a cheap product I've found that contains BH2 is "Norival" - it has tyrosine plus l-biopterin - the tyrosine might be a bust for you though - do your own research, I am \*not\* a doctor and could be dead wrong... I am also not ill, so can handily survive most experimentation while you will have to be quite careful -

For diet - I would eat lots of small/mercury free seafood like sardines/oysters - like daily - great for your brain, it has electrons and they contain lots of RNA:
[http://warddeanmd.com/ribonucleic-acid-part-one/](http://warddeanmd.com/ribonucleic-acid-part-one/)
[http://warddeanmd.com/ribonucleic-acid-part-two/](http://warddeanmd.com/ribonucleic-acid-part-two/)

This is my latest biohack - Yasko uses RNA formulas but they are pricey - they're just nucleotides, so I figured why not take a bunch of free nucleotides and let the body put them where it wants? Plus, Dr. Benjamin Frank used them in his research - and ate lots of sardines
[http://www.amazon.com/Life-Extensio...pc&ie=UTF8&qid=1401462146&sr=1-2&keywords=rna](http://www.amazon.com/Life-Extension-RNA-Capsules-Count/dp/B005XK9J2W/ref=sr_1_2?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1401462146&sr=1-2&keywords=rna)

So you're a nutritionist - that's great - you might want to start supporting that mitochondria - CO Q10 is also a methyl donor FYI - d-ribose/mag/ALA/ whatever the awesome foursome that Dr. Steven Sinatra talks about -

The sun thing is quite worrisome - my kid can't handle the sun and neither can my husband or sister-in-law - so this is my logic on that: sun sensitivity means you don't have enough zinc - zinc is used to convert B6 to P5P - P5P is used to convert glutamates to GABA - naturally I could be wrong - but I recently figured out that my kid has a problem with the pyroxidal kinase enzyme which does that particular conversion - I did that with an amino acid test where I looked at all the metabolites that were high and figured out what wasn't being used and what cofactors were required...

You can also use a product called: Zinc Status which will use a taste test to see if you are low on zinc - the theory is that if it tastes awful, you need it...
[http://www.amazon.com/Ethical-Nutri...F8&qid=1401462038&sr=1-1&keywords=zinc+status](http://www.amazon.com/Ethical-Nutrients-Zinc-Status-Liquid/dp/B000Q3ZRL8/ref=sr_1_1?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1401462038&sr=1-1&keywords=zinc+status)

I like Concentrace Minerals - it's a liquid - but it tastes awful...

Here is a great place to learn about methylation:
[http://www.heartfixer.com/AMRI-Nutrigenomics.htm](http://www.heartfixer.com/AMRI-Nutrigenomics.htm)

Now, getting back to your Lyme, I googled "pleomorphic lyme" and got the following hit on a google book:
[http://books.google.com/books?id=Bt...oQ6AEwBg#v=onepage&q=pleomorphic lyme&f=false](http://books.google.com/books?id=Btj4KghQcDQC&pg=PA80&lpg=PA80&dq=pleomorphic+lyme&source=bl&ots=2PgRdId_FM&sig=pTbFoOKC-4k_BqZVOv9lR97gtUY&hl=en&sa=X&ei=jvSHU9iwI8a0yATwtoGAAQ&ved=0CEoQ6AEwBg#v=onepage&q=pleomorphic%20lyme&f=false)

He chats about lyme and its biofilms which are quite difficult to get rid of - but my feeling is you can at least improve the quality of your life by killing some bugs, improving your detoxification system (methylation) and improving your immune system - a little bit at a time - and if you keep at it, you will eventually get better -

And now I would like to introduce you to a lecture by a woman who spent something like 50 years studying pleomorphic bacteria and was nominated for a Nobel Prize for her work - she actually cultured them using live blood and some stains - amazing woman:

She thought that all disease was caused by these stealth pathogens and this lady came to the same conclusion:
[http://www.heidinotes.com/research.html](http://www.heidinotes.com/research.html)

Now, they are injecting people with MS with hookworms and it is halting the disease in its tracks... why is that? What if your body was full of jackals and you needed Simba to keep them all at bay... as that article above states, many people with MS figure out they have Lyme and leave the MS forums to go to the Lyme forums... and maybe the hookworm would keep the Lyme at bay...

[http://www.nationalmssociety.org/Symptoms-Diagnosis/Other-Conditions-to-Rule-Out/Lyme-Disease](http://www.nationalmssociety.org/Symptoms-Diagnosis/Other-Conditions-to-Rule-Out/Lyme-Disease)

Think about Inger on this site - she eats raw mackeral head shakes... no parasites there, right? Maybe we need more bugs, not less... I mean look what these guys eat:
[https://www.facebook.com/humanfoodproject](https://www.facebook.com/humanfoodproject)

They'll kill an animal and eat its stomach contents - then they'll clean the intestines and stick them in a fire for like a minute and eat that... they'll go back to there village and touch the villagers and no one washes their hands... they drink from puddles on the ground... no one is sick from that - maybe cleanliness is not next to Godliness after all

Josh on this site also injected himself with hookworms and now has no allergies - gee I guess that improved his immune system a bit...

Klinghardt has \*lots\* of info on Lyme/parasites:

Lyme disease is mentioned as are glutamates - it's a short 9 minute TED talk that could blow your mind about how this woman who is a biochemist cured her kid of autism just by \*deleting\* glutamates and adding nutritious food in the way of raw smoothies - there are like 23 things in her smoothies - for instance, pumpkin seeds are antiparasitical, ginger is antimicrobial, coconut is antifungal/viral/microbial - she says something fascinating which really resonated with me and that is our microbial genetics far exceeds our own... check it out - I am sucking down one right now - it has 10 things in it including about 6 different herbs - you can't believe the nutrition in herbs - parsely/cilantro - look at Terry Wahls - what she did was astounding - I also started a garden - I only wish I knew how to amend the soil so it had a boatload of nutrients - I still remember my grandmother's white enamal pot sitting on the kitchen linoleum floor filled with egg shells and coffee grounds... she lived to be 97... he lived to be 98 - she made my grandfather eat garlic every day...

The other thing I noticed about your glutamate-fest and diet is you are eating bone broths - these are laced with glutamates moreso than stock - which also has some -

I personally am ready to sh%tcan all these pills and just start feeding the good flora some plant food - I mean everyone who is in microbiology these days says you can change your gut microbiome with plant food/fiber in just 2 weeks - American Gut says that and even the Yasko/Mullan contingent says that - there is thyme and cinnamon in Dirk Pearson's anti pylori pill - I mean the right food just totally rocks IMHO -

IMHO we could truly drive ourselves nuts with trying to treat our biochemistry like it was some sort of software flow chart - personally I'm tired of biochemisty and think I'm going back to lifestyle changes that give me a peace, food that I can use, and friends who help me grow - good on ya moving to the countryside and trying to go natural - I have a book called Herbal Remedies by Andrew Chevallier - small book - you can't believe the elixers you can make with herbs - variety is the key and keeping it raw is also important for the enzymes - eat a balanced diet and it should all even out...

Josh is correct - we are stuck in our paradigms - we see our world view from where we are - well what if we're buried in a hole?

There is some event that happened to you 3 years ago that sent your immune system into a tailspin - one of my friends broke both elbows rollerblading and got fibromyalgia - my own kid suffered from a 30 straight day migraine... that was also glutamates - it took him a year to stop blacking out - when your immune system is in the toilet, all kinds of pathogens then take over - who knows, maybe you had Lyme for years and didn't even care because you had an immune system...

And one more thing about glutamates you may or may not know - you will be constipated If you are, you need GABA - another sign -

So try to destress, eat a variety of nutritious food, relax and get well - stories abound of people curing themselves of cancer by doing just what you are doing

In a nutshell, people with low zinc might have pyroluria - which makes for a whole host of issues as zinc is involved in 300 different processes in the body -

He said he thought like a bug and what would be the smartest thing for a bug to do to evade the immune system - and he came up with what happens in pyroluria - no zinc, no P5P, no immune system, no glutathione - quite brilliant, really -

So, his theory is to add tons of zinc/B6/manganese/biotin to cure the pyroluria first which displaces the heavy metals which are there because the zinc is not, detoxify as lots of metals will release and then go after the Lyme/mycoplasma with a few herbs... he mentioned this guy who has a few books out on herbs and bacteria, herbs and viruses and herbs and lyme:
[http://www.amazon.com/Healing-Lyme-...TF8&qid=1402370113&sr=1-2&keywords=lyme+herbs](http://www.amazon.com/Healing-Lyme-Disease-Coinfections-Complementary-ebook/dp/B00CWD7W2C/ref=sr_1_2?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1402370113&sr=1-2&keywords=lyme+herbs)

Naturally, EMF makes the mycotoxins grow exponentially as he did an experiment where he put a Farraday cage over one Petri dish and the petri dish that was not covered grew 600 times more bacteria -

the powerpoint for the presentation is here:

You could quite possibly have Gilbert's Syndrome - impaired glucoronidation - i.e. another way the liver detoxifies - the hot ticket for that is calcium d-glucarte - you can read about it here:
[http://alwayswellwithin.com/resources/wellness/gilberts-syndrom/](http://alwayswellwithin.com/resources/wellness/gilberts-syndrom/)

And of course, the pubmed study which shows people with Gilbert's have 40% less ability to clear lorazepam - the active ingredient presumably in Ativan...
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7836548](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7836548)

One other thing you might want to consider - clostridia make glucoronidase - the enzyme which cleaves off the glucose molecule your liver attaches to the toxin to excrete it - thus even if you don't have Gilbert's, if you have lots of clostridia, they will be generating glucoronidase which will effectively screw up that live detox pathway - taking one pill will last around 5 hours - so twice a day would cut it for most people - Dave Asprey also has a nice writeup on it -

[http://www.thorne.com/altmedrev/.fulltext/7/4/336.pdf](http://www.thorne.com/altmedrev/.fulltext/7/4/336.pdf)

Welcome to my party... sigh.

Now, getting back to some words of wisdom from Klinghardt that hit home for me personally... he says the first born gets the most mercury from the mother - so that means I got my mother's mercury and that coupled with all the amalgams I had when I was young from the crooked dentist we went to made me quite hyper... so, I have a baby - and another one and noticed that "pregnancy was the only thing that slowed me down"... but I never knew until 2 days ago that it was probably because I unburdened my mercury load on my first born... who was SO hyper kinetic when he was young...

The other cool thing he talked about was parasites - he says if you don't look for them within 20 minutes of passing a stool they release an enzyme and self-destruct - this is why parasite testing is such a joke in this country... I also know from experience that a full moon makes them active - maybe they even lay their eggs then - don't remember - but every time there is a full moon, I send some Paragone down the hatch just for grins...

As for Jack Kruse, I 100% know he is correct about electricity and the body... and for the craziest of reasons... when I was a kid, a geologist came to talk to talk to us - he was exploring a cave and a 2000 pound boulder rolled over him and crushed his organs - naturally they called the priest and a few other members of the church and they "layed their hands" on him... well, the doctors could not understand how his organs had regenerated... and it was touted as "a miracle"... years later when I lost my faith, it still was in the back of my mind because I have known this guy for like 40 years as well as everyone in that hospital room - and I couldn't reconcile how that could have happened...

Well, I started reading Becker and his "dying muscle is positively charged and regenerating muscle is negatively charged" and then there is Jack Kruse talking about 30000 or was it 30000 volts across the mitochondria and what I thought about was the priest always wore these leather shoes... the man cured lots of people - shingles was one of the things I remember - and I laughed

When life's little mysteries unfold, it is such a kick to me and so is helping someone regain their health - anyone with Lyme is not thinking clearly as the bugs are eating away their brain - they want a simple recipe as to how to get well - so take the herbs mentioned on Bruhner's page in the doses he recommends - resveratrol is one of Dr. K's top 10 supplements anyway - and feel better -

feel better soon and don't give up!

# Thread: Tiny Bubbles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2014-05-26T21:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-128426>

Tiny bubbles everywhere! Thousands of them! And more are forming by the minute! What to do??
Sorry, getting ahead of myself. Here's the story:

Thanks to DH, and the oversight of the previous owner, I now have an outdoor CT tank out in the country. It is thick metal, round, and looks to be a cover for some sort of machinery. We were told it was originally intended to be a watering trough, but nothing ever happened with it. DH pulled it out of the ground, where it was mostly buried, upside down. He brushed off the rust, and painted it. It makes a perfect tub, large enough for two. It has a nice lip on the edge, great for resting the head, or sitting. The depth is perfect for me.

We put it in a spot that was half shade, half sun. Filled it with water from our wonderful deep well. So nice for CT. The water wasn't real cold like for regular CT, but it still gave me goosebumps. Felt fabulous. I lay there, soaking up the sun for a bit, then I noticed lots of bubbles on my arms. Or the hairs on my arms, actually. Some hairs had a string of bubbles on them; I could count 5 to 8 bubbles on some hairs. After more time, looking at my shoulder and upper arm, there was a forest of bubbles. So cool! And they clung fairly tightly. I could swish an arm or leg gently in the water, and most would still cling, though others let go and floated to the surface. Bubbles also clung down the walls of the tank.

Of course I thought of Pollack & Kruse & Ling. All those EZ droplets, sending me energy. No, I couldn't feel anything specific. The next day I bathed in the same water from the day before. The water was definitely warmer. Goosebumps were subdued. The sunshine was about the same, partly cloudy. The bubbles were diminished. But since the hairs weren't clogged with bubbles, I could see that yes, there were bubbles on my skin as well. Minuscule ones! Made my skin look shiny. With patience, I did grow some strings of bubbles on some hairs. Bubbles on the tank walls again.

The third day, I kept the same water again. Partly cloudy again, same temp as the day before, as far as I could tell, and lots more bugs and debris, lol. Goosebumps subdued again. Bubbles were even slower forming than the day before. Finally got some sheen to the skin, which were bubbles upon close inspection. Only a few bubbles on hairs. Bubbles only on a couple of inches of tank wall just below the surface.

My first guess is that it isn't the water temperature that is the variable, but the "age" of the water. When it is drawn fresh from the well, I think it is more energetic. But I think, in the interests of science, I need to float away more time to figure this all out. Who knows, maybe its the tiny bugs?

Also, if we are able to get energy in this manner, through the hairs, is that a reason why the arm and leg hair is generally thicker the further it is from the brain? Maybe our hair serves as a DC voltage amplifier?

> The continued flow of this DC electron current induces the water hydration shell that surrounds collagen. It uses the electric power in the quanta of the electron to make collagen hydrophilic so water binds to it maximally. Oh neat, Inger! When I go back to the city to CT in the tub, I have never noticed any. I think it is all the fluoride in the tapwater. And the tub is indoors, away from sunshine.

### nonchalant — 2014-05-27T15:33:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-128560>

> [ssj3 said:](/goto/post?id=128554)
>
> Thought process... gases captured in the water form a hydrophobic surface with the water (bubble) and the cooled water increases the area of stability and structure. And since the tub since it is connected to the earth it is collecting negative electrons further increasing the structured EZ.

It's impossible to visually tell the difference between a bubble and an underwater droplet. Pollack's book tells of the ways they used to determine that the "vesicles" were not bubbles. They punctured them with a sharp probe, and usually it had no effect on the droplets. They also squeezed and pulled them, but the droplets regained their original shape quickly. They even cut some in half, but the droplet instantly reformed.

He said both bubbles and droplets have EZ 'membranes'. And he said that the structured water inside droplets can undergo a phase transition as pressure changes (like when I move my leg from the bottom of the tank to near the surface) or as they absorb more sunshine (or body heat?), and become a gas bubble. Phase transition -- Jack's favorite term! I do think the metal tank grounding may have something to do with all the bubbles. And water straight from the well definitely could have dissolved gasses.

You have given me an idea, ssj3. Next time we go out to the country, I should bring a needle with me to the tank. Do my own experiment with the larger bubbles.

### HoneyChild — 2014-05-27T16:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-128562>

I've always had the bubbles when in an indoor city bathtub, maybe because the bathroom faces east so gets sunlight in the morning.

### Inger — 2014-05-27T18:31:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-128580>

### nonchalant — 2014-06-10T19:51:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-130383>

This weekend I got a chance to play with bubbles again. This time I took my needle!
When the bubbles got big enough, I tried puncturing them with the needle. Not as easy as it sounds. They evaded the needle, moving to the side, or releasing from the skin to float to the surface (lower density). Tough membrane. I found that approaching them from above was the way to go. I could not pop any of the bubbles. The needle just went inside with little reaction from the droplet. Removing the needle also had no effect. I also managed to slice into them, using the side of the needle. No popping, no dividing into two.

This time I also tried smashing some that were attached the inside of my elbow using quite a bit of force. Tough little buggers.
DH joined me for a while. When I pointed out all these neat features of the bubbles, he just looked at me and said "You must not have much to do while soaking in the tank."

### nonchalant — 2014-06-10T23:11:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-130416>

And also, when I managed to trap a bubble under the point of the needle, the surface of the bubble would deform a bit before the needle finally punched through.

### nicld — 2014-06-11T04:38:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-130476>

I noticed it too when I did CT outside today, very interesting. It was also pretty neat to see them on the goosebumps.

### nonchalant — 2014-06-11T05:39:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-130488>

Aren't they cute Nicole?
Back in the city, doing CT in the indoor tub, I was able to see a few bubbles. Very, very few, but they seemed to be the same thing. I usually put a few drops of iodine into my fluoridated tap water.

### sjoshua — 2014-06-18T19:42:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-131278>

I noticed a huge difference in the number of bubbles that form along the walls of my dogs' water dishes (that sit outside, on the ground) immediately after changing my home RO filters. me thinks it has to do with how able the water is to become structured

Fluoride be damned (...in the water supply)!

### nonchalant — 2014-07-01T04:53:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-132796>

A few days ago I held a hair underwater in my tank. It was about a foot long. Eventually bubbles formed along its entire length, and they grew in size as time passed. Looked like a string of pearls.  I swished the hair around, being careful not to let it touch itself, and the bubbles clung tightly. Then I let the end of the hair (an inch or two) float up to the surface. The bubbles (approximately 10 of them) stayed where they were, just below the surface, still attached to the hair. Then I pulled the hair back down, and those bubbles let go and stayed under the surface, and soon popped. Finally I stretched both ends of the hair, and the bubbles still clung, though they pulled apart from each other. When I let go, and the hair snapped back, every single bubble let go.

### nonchalant — 2014-07-16T00:54:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-134513>

Are there tiny bubbles in the ocean? Salt water diminishes the EZ. The sodium ion invades the lattice. But there is EZ left still? Pollack's book p97 says the "balance point will depend on how much energy enters the system: more intense incident energy yields larger exclusion zones". So there should be EZs in seawater, at least near volcanic vents or where sunlight is strong. Is that why artificial reefs are attractive? A shipwreck lands in relatively shallow water, and sea life is quickly attracted. I thought it was the anchoring points, but perhaps the ship's hydrophyllic surfaces structures sea water? Life would be attracted to the energy.

### nonchalant — 2014-07-16T04:40:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-134551>

Dr K has said that there are EZs in blood vessels, with the protons flowing through the center of the vessels. That's a fairly salty environment.

### PaulG — 2014-07-16T07:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-134567>

Sally, you are definitely onto something with the bubbles. I seek out bubbles whenever I go to the ocean by swimming/floating in turbulent water or walking in the surf as this gives me amazing energy. I'm thinking of adding a pump to my bath so I can do CT in bubbles, I know others use spa's for their CT and using a pump would counteract the warming layer effect that occurs when you lie motionless. I guess structured water works both externally and internally.

### Sue-UK — 2014-09-03T18:31:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tiny-bubbles.10926/post-139832>

When I first started outside CT with well water, I didn't notice bubbles, even with fresh well water and sunlight. I've had the sense recently that although I'm changing shape I haven't been shedding fat, it's been more about getting rid of biologic concrete (glycation) first ..... And seeing bubbles form makes me think my skin is now becoming more normal in a hydrophilic sense .....

### Jason B — 2014-05-27T00:18:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wellnessfx.10927/post-128442>

Hey everybody,

I'm planning on getting my initial labs done very soon and am considering WellnessFX. Anybody have any experience with them or suggestions for a better option? I'm in Los Angeles.

I've been eating "paleo" or "bulletproof" for about a year and a half now, but I have yet to get any labs done. Excited to see what's really going on in there! Thanks.

Jason

### kyrakitty — 2014-06-18T09:29:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wellnessfx.10927/post-131239>

If I hadn't found a physician to do labs for cheaper I would have done them through WellnessFX. But I was feeling so crummy I had to find a physician to help me.

If you feel relatively healthy and are simply trying to tweak yourself so that your labs are in the optimal range then WellnessFX is a good start. But at some point you might require hormones or other labs that can only be ordered by having a functional medical doctor.

I live 40 min from WellnessFX so everyone around here is always talking about it.

Hi,

In one of the first webinars I listened to on this website, I heard Jack talk about "quality" erections being a sign of good super conductivity. I am sorry I can't remember what webinar it was in. I believe Jack stated that for women, the quality of orgasms is a good bio marker of super conductivity.

I just repeated this to get everyone's attention to get a discussion going about super conductivity. As I have contemplated so much on this website, I believe what we may all be shooting for is super conductivity. Is that our goal? I know when I get out of the ice bath, I am reminded of how I felt when I was 17. Each time I get out of the ice, I find my state to be reminiscent of how I felt all the time at one point in my life.

Does youth equal super conductivity? Is anti -aging super conductivity?

I hope this to be the beginning of an exciting super conductive thread.

Mel

### Josh — 2014-05-27T06:31:48+0300

>

You know the joke, shall I repost it here???

A famous Soviet academic, Petya, is dying, and his friend and colleague, Vasya, comes to his
bedside to pay his last respects to this great old man. He finds Petya Lying there with closed eyes
and a sad look on his face.
Slowly he opens his eyes, looks at his friend, and then says, ”Vasya, look at all those books on that
wall over there – just look at them.”
”Yes, Petya,” replies his friend. ”Those are your most famous works. Everyone knows what an
important and...”
”Yes, yes, yes!” interrupts Petya. ”But look over there at that wall – you see all those books?”
”Yes, my dear friend. Those are the translations of your works into all the languages of the...”
”Yes, but that is not the point. Look over there – do you see those books?”
”But of course. Those are the works of your students and followers. We all admire...”
”No, but you don’t understand, Vasya!” exclaims Petya. ”Look, do you remember when we were
students, and we were sent off to work on the collective farm?”
”Of course I remember, my friend. How could I forget?”
”And do you remember, Vasya, walking down that country road one hot summer’s day?”
”Yes, Petya, yes I do.”
”And do you remember, we passed that cornfield, and there, sitting on the top of one of the
haystacks, was that pretty young Masha?”
”Yes, yes, Petya, I do! You liked her, I remember!”
”Yes,” says Petya, sinking back onto his pillow with a blissful smile on his face. ”You know what I did,
Vasya? I climbed up on top of the haystack and we were really starting to have a bloody good time.
But you know, my friend, those bloody haystacks – just when you are really getting somewhere,
whoosh!... they give way under you. I did everything I could, but every time that bloody haystack...
So you see, I have just been thinking, Vasya” – Petya looked round the room – ”how it would have
been if we had all these bloody books underneath us!”

My most interesting sexual experiences have not had much to do with erections or performing. I participated in a fair amount of so-called "tantra" work in my late 20's. It was starting"cool" without strong arousal and staying with breathing, contact and consciousness. I could hangout with a partner, pretty much any partner, in an orgasmic space for hours without ejaculation. It exposed some really hidden parts of me. It was perhaps too early for me to go there. Around the same time, I started to find the same experience in meditation which is less work. It was not the end of sex, and it has never been the same after that time. The possibilities beyond the physical experience in "sexual" meetings are really extraordinary in the right context and to go there exposes pretty much everything....

As I understand it, true "tantra" is only possible after one has no more desire, so I am not there and it is not a goal for me. Once meditation or whatever I have been experiencing consumes you, sex looks like one of the many doors.....

Of course it could all just be low free T.....

Something to do with this:

> **Abstract**
> Learning in the brain requires complementary mechanisms: potentiation and activity-dependent homeostatic scaling. **_We introduce synaptic scaling to a biologically-realistic spiking model of neocortex_** which can learn changes in oscillatory rhythms using STDP, and show that scaling is necessary to balance both positive and negative changes in input from potentiation and atrophy. We discuss some of the issues that arise when considering synaptic scaling in such a model, and show that scaling regulates activity whilst allowing learning to remain unaltered.

[http://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-642-37213-1\_3](http://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007%2F978-3-642-37213-1_3)

Seems to be dependent upon the binding of glutamte to NMDA receptors which allows Ca2+ into the cell to signal more stuff. Too much and not enough create problems....Body seems to regulate both influx of Ca2+ and Mg+ with NMDA receptors and regulates the number of receptors if necessary and possible to manage intracellular homeostasis...Mitochondria also have NMDA receptors...

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-21T08:08:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-successes.10932/post-135043>

People just do not understand how CT works. It is physics. Cations, like K+, may induce strong cooperative hydrogen-bonding around them due to the polarization of water O-H by cation-lone pair interactions. Gilbert Ling was the first person to realize the importance of cations actions to making water act coherently. The movement of these cations is what causes water to alter its molecular structure by altering hydrogen bonding networks. This changes its ability to carry energy and information directly alters its physiologic abilities.

The effect is reinforced by additional polarization effects and the resonant intermolecular transfer of O-H vibrational energy, mediated by dipole-dipole interactions and the hydrogen bonds in water. Reorientation of one molecule induces corresponding motions in the neighbors in water.

Thus solute molecules can 'sense' these changes via changes in the network. They do it by reacting to the effect each other's solubility in this water. This directly changes the hydrogen bonding network angles. Network effects reach each other at distances of several nanometers and surfaces may have effects extending to tens of nanometers. This long range correlation of molecular orientation has recently been confirmed using hyper-Rayleigh light scattering and is a reason for the **high [dielectric constant](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/microwav2.html#dielectric) of water.** When _temperature is raised_ there is a **[reduction in this dielectric constant](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/data.html#Dielectric_constant)** and the number of _hydrogen bonds is reduced._ When cold thermogenesis acts on water it **increases its dielectric constant**, increases the number and strength of the hydrogen bonding network and this allows for a greater amount of energy to be transferred to and fro on water networks in a cell. When you understand these concepts well you begin to see why Cold thermogenesis is not a hormetic effect. This is why the ancestral thought leaders are way off base on many of their beliefs. It is a natural effect based upon the physical laws in nature. This is why cold is always associated with survival and increased semiconductive currents in life.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-07-21T13:19:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-successes.10932/post-135056>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=135043)
>
> People just do not understand how CT works. It is physics. Cations, like K+, may induce strong cooperative hydrogen-bonding around them due to the polarization of water O-H by cation-lone pair interactions. Gilbert Ling was the first person to realize the importance of cations actions to making water act coherently. The movement of these cations is what causes water to alter its molecular structure by altering hydrogen bonding networks. This changes its ability to carry energy and information directly alters its physiologic abilities.
>
> The effect is reinforced by additional polarization effects and the resonant intermolecular transfer of O-H vibrational energy, mediated by dipole-dipole interactions and the hydrogen bonds in water. Reorientation of one molecule induces corresponding motions in the neighbors in water.
>
> Thus solute molecules can 'sense' these changes via changes in the network. They do it by reacting to the effect each other's solubility in this water. This directly changes the hydrogen bonding network angles. Network effects reach each other at distances of several nanometers and surfaces may have effects extending to tens of nanometers. This long range correlation of molecular orientation has recently been confirmed using hyper-Rayleigh light scattering and is a reason for the **high [dielectric constant](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/microwav2.html#dielectric) of water.** When _temperature is raised_ there is a **[reduction in this dielectric constant](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/data.html#Dielectric_constant)** and the number of _hydrogen bonds is reduced._ When cold thermogenesis acts on water it **increases its dielectric constant**, increases the number and strength of the hydrogen bonding network and this allows for a greater amount of energy to be transferred to and fro on water networks in a cell. When you understand these concepts well you begin to see why Cold thermogenesis is not a hormetic effect. This is why the ancestral thought leaders are way off base on many of their beliefs. It is a natural effect based upon the physical laws in nature. This is why cold is always associated with survival and increased semiconductive currents in life.

From -- [http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/explan3.html#diel](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/explan3.html#diel)

_Polar molecules, where the centers of positive and negative charge are separated, possess [dipole moment](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/microwav2.html). This means that in an applied electric field, polar molecules tend to align themselves with the field. Although water is a polar molecule, its hydrogen-bonded network tends to oppose this alignment. The degree to which a substance does this is called its dielectric constant ([permittivity](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/microwave.html)).

**Because water possesses a hydrogen bonded network that transmits polarity shifts extensively through rapid and linked collective changes in the orientation of its hydrogen bonds, it has a high dielectric constant.** This allows it to act as a solvent for ionic compounds, where the attractive electric field between the oppositely charged ions is [reduced by about 80-fold](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/microwav2.html), allowing thermal motion to separate the ions into solution

On cooling, as the water network strengthens and water's dipole moment increases, the [dielectric](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/data.html#Dielectric_constant) of liquid water climbs to 87.9 (at 0 °C), increasing on conversion to ice then increasing further as the ice is cooled.

On heating, the dielectric constant drops, and liquid water becomes far less polar, down to a value of about 6 at the [critical poin](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/data.html#criticalpoint)t (647.096 K)_​

You want your water to have a strong hydrogen bonding network, which can "transmit energy better". A more dispersed network is a weaker network, which is one that can contain less information (I'm thinking qubit superpositions now ...)

Within about 5 minutes of getting into a 12 deg C bath my temperature went from 98 deg F to 99.2 deg F
Body temperature was steady for about 30 minute. For the next 15 minute (through 45 minutes total) my temperature dropped to 98.8 deg F as the bath temperature cooled to 9 deg C.

I think this is a good sign that I have some BAT. I do feel warmer throughout the day. Just want to wear sleeveless tops. Also notice I don't shiver quite as much after CT, but that could be because it is summer.

Does anyone else have temperature values to compare with mine?

# Thread: Electron overload via semiconduction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electron-overload-via-semiconduction.10937/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-05-28T11:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electron-overload-via-semiconduction.10937/post-128708>

What happens when the amount of electrons into our bodies exceeds our ability to utilise them??

Dark skin is an example of an evolutionary photoprotection mechanism due to excess electrons from the sun. In [Sunburn](http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/573740/sunburn) in order to limit epidermal damage, the [pigment](http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/460189/pigment) [melanin](http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/373722/melanin) (which is produced by epidermal [cells](http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/101396/cell) called melanocytes) darkens through oxidation (nee [redox](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Redox))

For plants, it was previously thought that oxidation of Carotenoids was required but [in 2006](http://www.biodesign.asu.edu/labs/assets/lindsay/pdfs/Visoly_PR_PUBLIC.pdf) it was shown in an article called "[Conductance of a biological wire](http://www.pnas.org/content/103/23/8686.abstract)" that oxidation is not required and the electron overload can be handled in a neutral unchanged state (Ling's resting state!).

> We knew that biomolecules called carotenoids participate in this process of photoprotection, but the question has been, how does this work?” said Iris Visoly-Fisher, a postdoctoral research associate in the Biodesign Institute at Arizona State University.
>
> Carotenoids act as ‘wires’ to carry away the extra sunlight energy in the form of unwanted electrons, somehow wicking away the extra electrons across long distances from locations that could damage plant tissues and photosynthesis. During photoprotection, the consensus school of thought was that carotenoids—the source of the orange pigments in carrots and Vitamin A — become oxidized, or charged, losing an electron in the process.
>
> Now, Fisher and other ASU scientists have found a way to measure for the first time the electrical conductance within such an important biomolecule. And in doing so, the team has produced a new discovery which shatters the prevailing view. The research team found that oxidation is not required for photoprotection, but rather, carotenoids in a neutral, or uncharged state, can readily handle the electron overload from the sun.

So, the question remains how does the human body handle electron overload?

According to [wiki](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electron_acceptor) a **terminal electron acceptor eg oxygen** allows the cytochromes to transfer electrons out of the system to this molecule so there's not an electron overload. In **fermentation** electrons are removes from the NADH molecules reduced during glycolysis and transferred to a molecule that acts as the terminal electron acceptor. eg **lactic acid,ethanol, acetic acid, and butyric acid** which are the end products of fermentation.

This is all standard normal biochemistry but I am interested in abnormal extreme phenomena as it can open up interesting avenues.

Jack talked about what happens when a [supernova occurs](http://www.astronomycafe.net/qadir/q2954.html) and I was reminded of my morbid interest in [spontaneous human combustion](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spontaneous_human_combustion). (I did chuckle when I read the sentence below)

-   [Brian J. Ford](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brian_J._Ford) has suggested that [ketosis](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ketosis), possibly caused by [alcoholism](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alcoholism) or low-carb dieting, produces [acetone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Acetone), which is highly [flammable](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flammable) and could therefore lead to apparently spontaneous combustion.[\[18\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spontaneous_human_combustion#cite_note-Microscope-18)[\[19\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spontaneous_human_combustion#cite_note-19)

Could there be a quantum explanation to such violent implosions or does human wick theory explain all cases?

> Conduction of current in conductors is due to electrons only but semiconductors are really insulators that have been modified by the addition of dopants that produce lattice defects that enable the flow of current. When too many electrons are present we call it N type and when not enough are present we call it P type and these holes are the lattice positions where an electron was supposed to have been and they can move just as the electrons can move. usually both holes and electrons are present because of either deliberate design or flaw and both contribute to conduction.

From Beckers work

> Ongoing research has shown that bone has electrical properties. The bone matrix is a biphasic (two-part) semiconductor, i.e. a crystalline solid with an electrical conductivity. The collagen component of bone matrix is an N-type semiconductor and the apatite component a P-type. When tested for piezoelectricity, collagen turns out to be a piezoelectric generator while apatite is not. These function as two semiconductors, one an N-type, the other a P-type forming a PN-junction, which sets up a potential barrier and acts as an efficient rectifier, i.e. a semiconductor diode.
>
> Mechanical stress on the bone thus produces a piezoelectrical signal from the collagen. The signal is biphasic, switching polarity with each stress-and-release. The signal is rectified by the PN-junction between apatite and collagen. The strength of the signal tells the bone cells how strong the stress is, and its polarity tells them what direction it comes from. Osteogenic (bone forming) cells, which have been shown to have a negative potential, would be stimulated to grow more bone, while those in the positive area would stop production of matrix and be resorbed when needed. If bone growth and resorption are part of one process, the electrical signal acts as an analog code to transfer information about stress to the cells and trigger the right response. Hence, stress is converted into an electrical signal.
>
> An interesting property of PN-junctions of semiconductor diodes may be observed when current is run though the diode in forward bias, i.e. when there is a good current flow across the barrier. Some of the energy is turned into light and emitted from the surface and are therefore known as light-emitting diodes (LEDs). Researchers found that bone was an LED that required an outside source of light before an electric current would make it release its own light, and the light it emitted was at an infrared frequency invisible to us, but consistent.

I am not clear on the newly introduced concept of topological insulators and how they relate to n-type and p-type semiconductors via [doping mechanism](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/Hbase/solids/dope.html)

Sorry for the ramble

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-28T14:47:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electron-overload-via-semiconduction.10937/post-128715>

What happens when you have too many electrons? Rare..............but your mitochondria has the ability to uncouple ECT. Remember that from the early leptin blogs?

# Thread: "Detox Summit" Functional Medicine Postioning....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-summit-functional-medicine-postioning.10941/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-05-28T20:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-summit-functional-medicine-postioning.10941/post-128736>

> **Detox Summit set for August**
> May 27, 2014Food & Spirit | Engredea News & Analysis
>
> -   -   [EMAIL](http://newhope360.com/forward?path=node%2F750591)
> -   [COMMENTS](http://newhope360.com/business/detox-summit-set-august?NL=NH-07&Issue=NH-07_20140528_NH-07_322&YM_RID=%27email%27&YM_MID=%27mmid%27&sfvc4enews=42&cl=article_3#comments) [0](http://newhope360.com/business/detox-summit-set-august?NL=NH-07&Issue=NH-07_20140528_NH-07_322&YM_RID=%27email%27&YM_MID=%27mmid%27&sfvc4enews=42&cl=article_3#comments)
>
>     Online Detox Summit, presented by nutritionist and author Deanna Minich and the Institute for Functional Medicine, will feature experts on the role toxins in health, followed by a 21-day Detox Challenge.
>
>     **More About:**
>
>     -   [Herbs and Botanicals](http://newhope360.com/herbs-and-botanicals)
>
>
>     Functional Medicine nutritionist and author, Deanna Minich, PhD, in collaboration with The Institute for Functional Medicine (IFM), is pleased to announce an international, online Detox Summit featuring experts on the role of toxins in health that will take place during the first week of August of 2014, followed by a 21-day Detox Challenge that everyone can participate in during the month of September.
>
>     The word “detox” is a catch-all term that means many things to people: drug and alcohol detoxification, nutritional detoxification, or cleanses using food, smoothies, and juices. During this informational event, 30 experts will discuss all aspects of detoxification. These experts include physicians, other health professionals, and researchers who will provide the latest clinical approaches for identifying and encouraging the elimination of environmental toxins. In addition to treatment of the body’s toxin burden, the Detox Summit will address emotional/mental toxins accumulated in the course of relationships, stressful interactions, and life experiences. Experts in self-help will discuss their tried-and-true techniques for transformation.
>
>     This combination of experts from various areas of medicine, nutrition, and personal growth makes this Summit unique, while offering a well-rounded, credible approach to a much-debated issue. Summit spokesperson Deanna Minich, PhD, stated, “‘Detox’ is a buzz word, and it brings with it much confusion and misinterpretation, which is why I conceived The Detox Summit—to investigate the science and self-help behind this movement of health. The Detox Summit, with 30 experts like functional medicine physician and best-selling author, Mark Hyman, MD, nutrigenomics pioneer and founder of Functional Medicine, Jeffrey Bland, PhD, and neurologist and best-selling author, David Perlmutter, MD, plus many others, will help guide and inform the public on topics like endocrine disruptors and fertility, dental amalgams, laboratory tests for determining whether we are toxic, and why we should watch out for gluten and genetically modified foods.”
>
>     Participants will be able to follow up the Detox Summit with the Detox Challenge—working with IFM Faculty and IFM Certified Practitioners to put their knowledge and enthusiasm for detoxification into practice in their own lives.
>
>     Laurie Hofmann, CEO of IFM, stated, “The Institute for Functional Medicine is pleased to collaborate with Deanna Minich, PhD, on the upcoming Detox Summit and Detox Challenge planned for August and September 2014. Deanna has served as a passionate, creative, and deeply knowledgeable member of our Functional Nutrition development team and faculty. We are thrilled to collaborate with Deanna on the Detox Summit and Detox Challenge and look forward to the opportunity of unveiling some of IFM’s personalized nutrition and food plan suites as part of these two very important and exciting online events. We hope that clinicians and patients alike will seize this unique opportunity to participate in both the education-oriented Summit and the experiential/interactive Challenge.”
>
>     The Institute for Functional Medicine Medical Director, Patrick Hanaway, MD, comments, “Using food and nutrition to assist the body in detoxification may seem new to some, but has been a well-established practice in traditional healing systems and functional medicine practices. Increasing evidence in mainstream medical journals indicates the burden of toxicity on health. For example, new research indicates that chronic conditions like type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and obesity are all associated with environmental toxins in the body. We need to start looking at the root of chronic diseases rather than simply suppressing symptoms. Identifying the root cause of disease and treating with a personalized approach is what we embrace in Functional Medicine. Detoxification is one way to treat an important root cause of many diseases.”
>
>     The Detox Summit is an online event. Interested persons can sign up via detoxsummit.com. During the week of August 4 to 10, 2014, people who have registered for the event will have free access to audio recordings of cutting-edge interviews with 30 experts in the area of toxicity on everything from environmental toxins in food, air, and water to internally-generated toxins such as thoughts, emotions, and behaviors. Listeners will not only be informed of the perils of these poisons, but, more importantly, will be taught about breakthrough solutions to reduce and transform them for healthier living. More information on The Detox Challenge will be released this summer. Join The Detox Summit movement now to become informed by the world’s brightest minds on toxicity and what you can do to become healthy! Find us at detoxsummit.com and Facebook at facebook.com/TheDetoxSummit.
>

[http://thedetoxsummit.com/](http://thedetoxsummit.com/)
