# Thread: My first seafood broth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-06-05T16:22:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-129771>

So, I have decided to do my first ever seafood broth and this is my experience.

-   Went into wholesale seafood place and bought some mixed marina (scallops, prawns, mussels, squid, tuna)
-   Asked for something to make fish stock and was given three large pink snapper fish frames with heads on FOR FREE
-   Put fish frames into slow cooker, filled with water plus half cup vinegar and cooked on low for 10 hrs overnight
-   Following evening ran through whole lot through blendtec blender and now have large 5l saucepan of liquid and smashed bones etc in the fridge

I was thinking I could now drink this over the next 5 days and/0r add it to recipes such as soup. Its pretty strong smelling and tasting.

I have probably by-passed numerous steps to either make decent stock or soup so have ended up with **stoup** so what do I do next? Is it safe to do this?

SOS to Inger - MHS alert.
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/page-14](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/page-14)

Kids are grossed out and wife refuses to eat/drink fish heads. I just remember Sandy from [Monkey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monkey_\(TV_series\))

### Inger — 2014-06-06T09:08:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-129927>

Ok Paul, I have never made a broth like that  but here is what i think.

It is absolutely fine to drink, just make sure nothing is going to stick in your throat. That the bones are small enuf to get down easily. I also never use vinegar, might be it would taste better without? But you might need to hold your nose. I always do when I drink my raw fish head smoothie. I just cannot like it! But who cares...lol just get it down. After all, it is medicine!

When I make fish broth (I do not do it often because I mostly drink fish head smoothies) I just cook the bones add sea salt and some seaweed and then i sieve it and drink the broth that i find very yummy! No need to hold my nose then. But like you do there, blending the bones and all, cant be bad for us, just a bit yucky lol
But IDK how big the idea is to do that because that is why we cook the fish heads/bones so long to suck out the nutrients into the broth that we drink.

My fish head smoothies are raw, you see. They are made of fatty fish that is no good for broth (fish fat gets rancid by heating) so that is why i make the aw fish head smoothie. Mackerel and herring heads you cannot make broth of. It needs to be non fatty fish for that, but the fish monger knows that i think.

So... if i were you, I would just cook the broth next time and sieve it and give to family, they will like it i bet that way! You can also use it for fish soup etc.
And the broth you made now you might have to drink all by yourself...lol I do bet it is healthy!

### caroline — 2014-06-06T11:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-129942>

I really don't think MHS are that bad at all .... with fresh lemon and fresh herbs .....and a glug of wine afterwards!

I see wine is getting to be a theme.......

### Inger — 2014-06-06T12:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-129945>

i tried to take a sip of wine after my head smoothie a few days ago... iiii it was yucky lol fish head smoothie taste with wine.. ehhh

I need a spoon of wild lingon berries.. they are so strong tasting nothing else is felt

i must be a wuss

### PaulG — 2014-06-08T08:52:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-130147>

Enjoying the soup now. It is really tasty with tumeric and garam massalla. Another quiver in my bow to optimal - shame no-one else in this house is courageous enough to give it a try.

Next milestone - embrace the 'raw' culture..

### PaulG — 2014-06-08T08:58:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-130148>

> Inger said:
>
> They are made of fatty fish that is no good for broth (fish fat gets rancid by heating) so that is why i make the raw fish head smoothie. Mackerel and herring heads you cannot make broth of. It needs to be non fatty fish for that, but the fish monger knows that i think.

Inger, surely there is fat to some extent in all fish. How does fresh fish that is slow cooked end up becoming [rancid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rancidification)?

### yewwei.tan — 2014-06-08T09:26:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-130154>

Fish heads certainty make a really really tasty broth . For some reason, I personally like leaner varieties of fish to make broth too, but that's purely from a taste point of view. Recently, it's been lots of bonito tuna and snapper varieties.

Cooking Tataki style should be a good compromise on the road to raw. Gordon Ramsay shows you how (with lots of swearing of course) --

. Take away the extreme high heat, questionable cooking oils, and you basically have a chunk of fish that is at least 90% raw. I love a raw snapper fillet with crispy skin (basically only cook on the skin side for about 1 min). Pair it with some good sauce, and I think it's something that almost anyone can enjoy.

Then of course, there's ceviche and poke --

### Dali Dula — 2014-06-08T15:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-130165>

I suggest reading Harold McGee's On Food And Cooking. This is not a cookbook. It is a deep treatment of food and cooking that goes into chemistry. For flavor fish broth is recommended to cook only 30 -40 min. Or it gets bitter. I have found this to be true. Shellfish bodies can be cooked for hours however. This book is used as a textbook in culinary schools. Fascinating read.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-06-09T14:50:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-130268>

The culinary school in Poughkipsee uses this I believe.

### greentrees — 2019-12-04T02:34:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-283057>

"Fish stocks, or fumets (from the French for “aroma”), are also generally prepared in an hour or less, since longer simmering of fragile fish bones can dissolve calcium salts that then cloud the liquid and give it a chalky taste. Stocks are made with fish bones, skins, trimmings, and heads, which are an especially rich source of gelatin and flavor."

(p.216 On Food and Cooking)

Screw it, I'm gonna try 4-24hrs. Usually I do my own thing and find out why people do X instead lol. Bring on the bitter calcium salts

### Saichi — 2019-12-04T03:13:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-283060>

I do 24 hour fish head/body skeleton broths specifically to make the bones soft and edible. Pretty much the gills are the only part I don't eat.

### Katie Durham — 2019-12-04T14:54:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-283066>

The reason to use vinegar in bone broths is to help minerals leach from the bones, for extra nutrition. But you only need a slash, not half a cup.

### Marko Pollo — 2019-12-04T20:42:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-283092>

I dont know if it matters, but I add the vinegar first to broken bones and minimal liquid, so the ph drops and hopefully leaches out all the goodness. After an hour of simmer, i fill the pot with water , diluting the broth to continue simmering.
My grandma taught this to my mom in the 1950s.

### Anne V — 2019-12-04T23:50:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-seafood-broth.10999/post-283111>

> [Marko Pollo said:](/goto/post?id=283092)
>
> I dont know if it matters, but I add the vinegar first to broken bones and minimal liquid, so the ph drops and hopefully leaches out all the goodness. After an hour of simmer, i fill the pot with water , diluting the broth to continue simmering.
> My grandma taught this to my mom in the 1950s.

next time you make one , let me know , i ll come and taste

# Thread: Are you observant?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 62
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2014-06-05T16:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/post-129775>

I really should be sleeping .....but I have had so many light bulb moments tonite .....I am so restless and had to get up.
I have always been proud of myself that I am very detail oriented ....I always remember details of a meal or a conversation or what people are wearing etc. In the food business, details matter ..... how something tastes, how a plate looks, how everything marries together ...it always has to be a symphony.

So , while I have always been very observant ......have I been missing out? Have I just been sitting on the sidelines of my life?

Stella and I have been saying over and over that we don't remember hardly anything about our trip to Florida. I have asked Josh .. why can't I remember anything? He ,very wisely, has said - because you were really relaxed and enjoying the moment - you were having fun!

I had stayed overnite last week at a dear friends and in the morning it was so gorgeous we sat outside on the patio in our pajamas and had breakfast. A neigbour popped over \[something she never does\] and we spent the next three hours laughing and talking. One thing led to another and we were talking about oxy and I was telling them about ben wah balls etc. We had so much fun and it turns out I don't remember much of that conversation either and I don't remember talking about the balls at all! The reason it came up tonite is because they have been canvassing their friend about them!

So - it seems like I am not just sitting back and observing .... I am actually actively participating and living life ...and having way too much fun!

When I looked at Jeremy's webinar again tonite ...... it is all about touch and connecting and just plain living in the moment and being actively present. I got that the first time - but now I see that Jeremy and Dr. k. are living life on their terms - no one else's .. and they make no apologies.

You don't always make a connection for whatever reason and when you don't you just have to move on. When there is lots of positive energy it is simply amazing. Everything is in focus ...it is all very visceral.

I realise that I don't have to remember the details .....but I do remember and can still feel the essence. That is what is important.

I think we don't often make those deep connections .....so - when we do .... it is a very special gift to be treasured.

I think I don't really know where I am going with this ....... except that it is way better to be actively engaged and living in the moment ...and I think that is what I have learned here! the most important thing I have learned here!

sorry .... I should have stayed in bed!

nite all!

### Inger — 2014-06-06T09:19:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/post-129928>

I am not sure Audrey it is a good thing to not remember... at least too little... or lets say.. I would personally not like that  I wonder why you do not remember the trip.. maybe you just needed a "break"? Or you had REALLY much wine?  I can remember very well even if I had wine, but i never drink very much, just max half a bottle.

Look, if you do not remember stuff, it is like you have had no life  that would be sad? And how can we learn?

I just had a rough evening a week or so ago and i was so sad, and then I thought man how can i get this bad feeling away, darn. And then I started to think about my nephews... and they are way cute.. small angels.. and i immediately felt so good
i often do that. If i feel like i cant do something, i think about someone i know that is very courageous.... etc. It helps huge!!! I kind of take their "spirit" into me and my issue is not much present anymore. If i had no memories i could not do that. Makes me think memory is pretty important......

But maybe you needed that break, who knows. No rule without an exception?

### caroline — 2014-06-06T11:09:25+0300

### Inger — 2014-06-06T12:12:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/post-129944>

yes, we are lucky... kind of funny we both have the same experiences in a way, even if a different way but two broken hearts - but yes we are healing fast thanks to Jack, true healing

Want to hear my last magic?  Ok... it will also reveal how NOT far i have come  but i want to tell it anyways.

A few days ago when I come home from my weekend Norway trip my parents told me my brother that lives here (the converted one) had hit on my internet cable that goes down in the garden with a hammer. He said to my parents he could feel something bad come out from it, that is why he needed to destroy it (he feels internet is antichrist or something similar - hat he has learned from my parents that thought so too before i moved in). I got so angry... how can anyone do anything like that, destroy others stuff? So crazy.... i just talked with my parents who did not support him in doing that, my dad said he must not destroy my things. and I did not say anything to my brother about it, but thought i will ask him to buy a new one - they are not very cheap either.
sooo yesterday I all of a sudden got an insight. I will not say anything, i will buy the new cable myself or use another old one i have, and just forget about the whole thing. That seemed like the right thing to do... to just forgive him and never speak about it again.
Guess what... yesterday evening he had fixed my bike    and i did not ask him about it at all

i am sure he could feel it through the air i had forgive him and he wanted to do something nice to me

talk about magic

this story also shows my own illness, how come i did not see this from the beginning? Why did i get angry? I know he is not healthy yet either... and he thought he does the right thing....
i need to REALLY learn from this!!!! But learning seems painfully slow for this girl...

but anyways, the story ended well and that is what counts

to just be good to someone that hurts you is the hardest lesson for me to learn ever. it does not come naturally for me at all  I know consciously it is the way to go, but man is it hard to practice. but I am going to change it!

### caroline — 2014-06-06T13:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/post-129950>

Inger ... I am not sure about your lesson ....I have spent a life time trying to be the nice person....but that isn't really what it is ...for me. I needed to get a backbone and for once and for all - say what I was thinking.

so there is something that you speak of above ...but I think, for me, I was always kind of a coward in a way.
People need to have boundaries and show respect....I deserve that ...and so do you.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-06-06T14:00:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/post-129951>

I was thinking the exact same thing as Audrey but refrained to post as I'm not really sure of which 'way' is best... probably one that allows you to empower people without the need to being hurt in the middle. Difficult thing to accomplish as a human being with feelings...

### SeaHorse — 2014-06-06T14:07:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/post-129952>

You can't make somebody have boundaries….but you can make sure you have them by sensing/reading your environment and the people around you. Sometimes our beliefs about behaviour results in really poor boundaries…until we change our beliefs about who we are, we won't be able to set our boundaries accordingly….learning to let go of distressing actions from others is one thing….taking distress into our bodies and feeling we have no other options is very different….

If you find you are doing all the "absorbing" inward from others and no "reflecting" outward towards them, then it is time to question your beliefs….that is "doormat" territory

I think both of you are changing your beliefs about yourselves, so that leads to new ways of experiencing those around you.

### caroline — 2014-06-06T14:11:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/post-129954>

Hi Albert! It seems this retrospection continues .....

for me - getting a backbone - finally empowered me. It may not always be right - but it feels more honest.

I always figured maybe I don't know the whole situation etc. etc. etc. - but now I think I can't let people screw me over without standing up for myself ...if I get it wrong - they can tell me and then we go from there.

But, mostly, it should be done with love and respect ........if at all possible.... I just had a situation where I was brutally honest and used some really crap language .... but it was long, long, long overdue and I needed to make a really direct point with as few words as possible - so there was no doubt!
I also have to suffer the consequences - but I made that choice up front ....and no regrets.

The bottom line .... we all deserve to be treated with respect....

### Jack Kruse — 2014-06-06T15:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/post-129962>

Fun or wisdom requires collaboration between souls. Most do not engage one another to do it any longer. The beauty of collaboration between generations is that we combine strength with wisdom. Collaboration pulls talent together to make a difference. When you engage with people, you build your own insight into what’s being discussed. Peering succeeds because it leverages self-organization without boundaries. Collaborations are the black holes of knowledge regimes. Their very vacuity that is their strength. It does not entail the transmission of something from those who have to those who do not, but rather the setting in motion of a chain of unforeseen accesses giving ideas a ray of life.

### Inger — 2014-06-06T15:03:39+0300

### NeilBB — 2014-06-06T20:34:53+0300

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=129992)
>

I just had a vision of what's gonna happen when I get stopped:

Cop: What do we have here? Hot shot driving convertible on interstate with the top down. You pretty dumb, huh?

Neil: Well sir. I was just collecting some photons with my bald head...

Cop: What? With no sunscreen. You crazy boy? Haven't you heard? Sun'll kill ya. All doctors agree.

Neil: I'll try to remember that officer.

Cop: We're you aware you were exceeding the posted speed limit by a wide margin?

Neil: Well, I have an epi-paleo emergency sir.

Cop: You backtalking me boy?

Neil: No sir.

Cop: And what's that in the thermos there? Some kinda homemade moonshine?

Neil: Just bulletproof coffee with a half pound of grass fed butter sir. Would you like to taste it. It's wonderful.

Cop: Are you crazy? Half pound of butter?! Saturated fat is deadly boy. I don't know whether to slap you, arrest you, or take your dumb ass to the nearest hospital. And what's that over there boy? Some kind of bong? Drug paraphernalia?

Neil: No sir. That's a power lung device. It makes your respiratory muscles stronger when you just breathe through it.

Cop: Uh-huh, right... I want you to exit the car slowly and place your hands behind your back...

### Stella — 2014-06-06T21:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-observant.11000/post-130008>

Audrey, I know we have spoken about our time together in Florida and we still are trying to remember and piece together the details. But I do agree that when you get off your ass and really start living in the moment, details aren't so important. It's the feeling and the connection that is made with others that's so priceless.

Evolution of our person is what's happening right now. It is exciting to experience. For so long my brain wasn't working well, so memories aren't well preserved because of lack of electrons. But now plenty of electrons so that I can remember but I choose better to live and experience the joy of the moment and those loved ones that are part if my life.

It really is more fulfilling and exciting being an active participant in life. Another aspect of this, is that the better you feel, the happier you are and the more wonderful things, experiences and people you attract into your life. And it is a cycle that is playing on repeat in my life right now. Good experiences, good connections all leading to good feelings. Wow who knew the importance of electrons!!!

I can't wait for my future to unfold and develop right before me.

And that's how Stella got her groove back!!!!

Jeremy had to go to the hospital this week because he was feeling bad. We went out to dinner and he dropped that on me.............funny thing.......I told him that night you will be fine.......not as friend would.........as a stern doctor would. He has been innovating his life using seafood and cold to a greater degree. He texted me from the hospital and told me his FEV 1 went up over 20%........his doctors were shocked. He found out he had another problem unrelated to his CF that was making him ill that was quite minor.............he was stoked. He drove from the hospital directly to Destin...........sent me a pic from a 350 gallon tub packed with ice with him in it.

Adding value to people doesn't always show up in the present moment. Sometimes you get to see it when you have moved on from that area, person, and then they come into your present again. The things that they are doing, the places they have been and what they have accomplished comes out. Yet it started when you added value to them in some way in your past. So just keep innovating with your inner circle to better our world. Always reevaluate your caucus. Strive for perfect 10's, not comfortable 5's in your circle of influence! Jeremy is one of those innovations..........to my inner circle.

# Thread: CO2 and Plant Nutrient Density
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co2-and-plant-nutrient-density.11001/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-06-05T20:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co2-and-plant-nutrient-density.11001/post-129836>

[http://www.boulderweekly.com/articl...els-to-cut-key-nutrients-in-global-crops.html](http://www.boulderweekly.com/article-12866-rising-co2-levels-to-cut-key-nutrients-in-global-crops.html)

> New research studying 41 strains of six different crops on three continents that was published May 7 in the scientific journal,Nature, shows that as concentrations of atmospheric carbon dioxide increase, the nutritional content of key food crops will drop, exacerbating hunger woes for millions of people worldwide. In some crops, concentrations of nutrients could drop by as much as 10 percent when CO2 levels rise to 500 parts per million by mid-century.
>
> Harvard University public health researcher Samuel Myers says that the new research once and for all resolves questions about the impacts of CO2 on major food crops.
>
> “Already, two billion people are suffering from zinc and iron deficiencies. That equals 63 million life years lost,” Myers says, explaining that the grains most affected by increased greenhouse gas emissions are exactly the ones that provide those nutrients.
>
> “This is a huge public health problem today ... These are grains and legumes that people rely on for nutrition,” he says.
>
> “Humanity is conducting a global experiment by rapidly altering the environmental conditions on the only habitable planet we know. As this experiment unfolds, there will undoubtedly be many surprises. Finding out that rising CO2 threatens human nutrition is one such surprise,” he says........

"Finding out that rising CO2 threatens human nutrition is one such surprise,”

"Surprise"....Really!?!?!?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Fruits out of season
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rick — 2014-06-05T20:24:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-129843>

I would like to be able to scientifically support Jack's recommendations for only consuming fruit during the long light cycles. I understand this is a 'biological mismatch' and sugars and starches eaten in winter increase inflammation and oxidative stress, but - can anyone explain specifically the mechanism of how this inflammation occurs in the short light cycles please??

Thanks!

Rick

### nonchalant — 2014-06-05T20:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-129850>

Well, briefly, the high solar energy necessary to grow the fruit, is also necessary to use its high energy after consumption.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-06-05T22:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-129858>

Seasonal fruit corresponds to photons of that seasons pattern.......so in summer fruits have high sugar contents, high water contents, and low ATP value. Therefore when they are broken down to electrons the electrons enter cytochrome 1. An avocado is a fruit that grows in different seasonal contexts and its electrons enter at cytochrome 2. The amount of ATP generated is different. And the the amount of O2 made is increased because more electrons are delivered to O2 to reduce it. Summer time fruits do not raise electrons.......do not have high ATP........so they contain more H2O in them to offset the ATP loss. Water and ATP production are stochastically link by Gilbert Ling's work. ATP and water molecules form the hydration shells around proteins to generate energy and transfer it through the system. Water forms hydration shells around proteins. That shell determines how the protein can be bent, shaped, and it length. This alters its mass and this subjects it to E-mc^2. Mass equivalence is a thermodynamic issue not a calorie one.........Simple.

### nonchalant — 2014-06-05T22:09:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-129861>

^^^^^^^^Yeah, what he said. ;-)

### SeaHorse — 2014-06-05T22:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-129864>

He took the words right out of your mouth didn't he?!

### DavidL — 2017-07-15T21:42:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-218957>

Maybe this is the wrong thread but you said when UV is present, we don't make ATP from food electrons. Which blog post has this info?

### Clayton — 2017-07-15T22:22:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-218958>

UV induces nitric oxide release, NO binds to Cytochrome C Oxidase, this inhibits electron flow along proximal cytochromes then IR and Red spins the ATPase. One of the mechanisms you get less hungry in the sun

### Clayton — 2017-07-15T22:23:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-218959>

Carbs enter cytochrome 1, Fats at 2

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-15T23:48:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-218974>

Yep. Vermont 2017 101. The Jablonski diagram slide and the incident EMF slide I showed. Also in Scompy's podcast this week

### cmegan — 2017-09-27T22:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fruits-out-of-season.11002/post-224393>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=129858)
>
> Seasonal fruit corresponds to photons of that seasons pattern.......so in summer fruits have high sugar contents, high water contents, and low ATP value. Therefore when they are broken down to electrons the electrons enter cytochrome 1. An avocado is a fruit that grows in different seasonal contexts and its electrons enter at cytochrome 2. The amount of ATP generated is different. And the the amount of O2 made is increased because more electrons are delivered to O2 to reduce it. Summer time fruits do not raise electrons.......do not have high ATP........so they contain more H2O in them to offset the ATP loss. Water and ATP production are stochastically link by Gilbert Ling's work. ATP and water molecules form the hydration shells around proteins to generate energy and transfer it through the system. Water forms hydration shells around proteins. That shell determines how the protein can be bent, shaped, and it length. This alters its mass and this subjects it to E-mc^2. Mass equivalence is a thermodynamic issue not a calorie one.........Simple.

Hi Jack.... so does this mean that avocados can be eaten during the cold winter months in the northeast, that they wouldn't be a seasonal mismatch?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-28T04:11:39+0300

They don't grow there then.
